Pak Study MCQs · pages 351-400

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

Battle of Badr was ___ battle of Islam ?

Correct Answer: first
Show explanation
Explanation

All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

 mentioned in Surat Al-Imran

1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

Total: Muslim 313-317

Muhajirun: 82-85

Ansar: 231

Aws: 61

Khazraj: 170

2 horses

70 camels

**


2

Name the first Battle between Muslims and Kafirs in Which Muhammad(PBUH) took part?

Correct Answer: Ghazwa-e- Abwa
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Wadan also known as the  Battle of Abwa, was the first battle involving an Islamic army led by the Prophet Muhammad.

These caravan raids between 623 and 624 led to the Battle of Badr.

3

Treaty of Hudaybiyyah took place in March 628________.

Correct Answer: Dhu al-Qi’dah 6 AH
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (R.A).

The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was an event that took place during the time of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammad, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca 

Sulah e Hudaibiya is called Fatah Al-Mubeen in Surah Al-Fatah

Sulah e Hudaibiya Was Signed In The Year 628/ 6H.

Sule e Huabaibiya Was Signed For 10 Years.

The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah took place in March 628 Dhu al-Qi’dah

7 clauses did the treaty of Hudaybiyyah contain

Hudabia is the name of the tree.

Hazrat Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina

Suhail bin Amar signed the Treaty of Hudaibiya on behalf of the Quraish Mecca.

ND-21-5-2023


4

Where was Masjid e Zarar?

Correct Answer: Madina
Show explanation
Explanation

Masjid al-Dirar was a Medinian mosque that was erected close to the Quba Mosque

Masjid al-Ḍirār  was a mosque built by hypocrites in Medina 

It was later destroyed by the order of Prophet Muhammad (SAAW).

while he was returning from the Expedition to Tabouk (which occurred in October 630 CE).

5

نمازہ جنازہ میں کتنی تکبیرات ہوتی ہیں؟

Correct Answer: چار
Show explanation
Explanation

 نمازہ جنازہ میں صرف چار تکبیریں کہی جاتی ہیں۔ پہلی تکبیر کے بعد ثنا، دوسری کے بعد درود شریف، تیسری کے بعد دعا پڑھتے ہیں اور چوتھی تکبیر کے بعد سلام پھیر دیتے ہیں

6

Who is remembered as first Muslim socialist?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abuzar Ghaffari (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

Abū Dharr al-Ghifārī, a companion of Muhammad  ,  a principal antecedent of Islamic socialism.

 Hazrat Abuzar Ghaffari (RA) belonged to the Banu Ghifar, the Kinanah tribe.

He died in 652 CE, at al-Rabadha, in the desert east of Medina.

 Hazrat Abuzar Ghaffari (RA) was the fourth or fifth person converting to Islam, and a member of the Muhajirun.

 belonged to the Banu Ghifar, the Kinanah tribe

7

Show explanation
Explanation

Salman was a companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

First Persian who converted to Islam.

He was referred to by the kunya Abu Abdullah. 

Born: 568 AD, Jay

Died: Ctesiphon, Iraq

Full name: Rōzbeh

First Persian who converted to Islam.

9

Imam Ghazali was a famous _______

Correct Answer: Muslim Philosopher and Mystic
Show explanation
Explanation

Imam Ghazali was one of the most prominent and influential philosophers, theologians, jurists, and mystics of Sunni Islam.

Born: c. 1058 Tus, Iran, Seljuq Empire

Died : 19 December 1111 (aged 52–53) Tus, Iran, Seljuq Empire


10

Maqam e Mahmood means?

Correct Answer: Station of Praise
Show explanation
Explanation

Station of Praise. تعریف کا مقام

Maqam Mahmud is the highest station and honor given to no one else by Allah except to His final messenger and prophet Muhammad ﷺ. Additionally, he has been given Al-wasila (the highest station) in paradise which no one has except him.


11

The name of Prophet whose age is mentioned in Quran is ________

Correct Answer: Prophet Nuh (a.s)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Noah's preaching and prophethood spanned 950 years according to the Quran, Ahadith and Tafsir.

وَلَقَدۡ اَرۡسَلۡنَا نُوۡحًا اِلٰى قَوۡمِهٖ فَلَبِثَ فِيۡهِمۡ اَ لۡفَ سَنَةٍ اِلَّا خَمۡسِيۡنَ عَامًا ؕ فَاَخَذَهُمُ الطُّوۡفَانُ وَهُمۡ ظٰلِمُوۡنَ‏ 
(29:14) We did indeed send Noah to his people and he lived among them a thousand years save fifty. Eventually the Flood overtook them while they were engaged in wrongdoing.
 Surah Al-'Ankabut 29:14-22

Territorial changes Arab rule is extended over modern-day Iraq

13

What is the atonement for breaking the fast.

Correct Answer: To Feed 60 people
Show explanation
Explanation

What is the punishment for breaking your fast during Ramadan?

Atonement means کفارہ

To atone for the intentionally missed/invalidated fast, a person must fast continuously for 60 days.

If they are unable to do that, they should feed 60 poor people.

What other name is given to Ghazwa-e-Ahzab:

Correct Answer: Ghazwa-e-Khandaq

Al-Ahzab the confederates, or "the clans", "the coalition", or "the combined forces")
The Battle of the Trench also known as the Battle of Khandaq and the Battle of the Confederates
was a 27-day-long defence by Muslims of Yathrib (now Medina).

16

Show explanation
Explanation

Tafsir Al-Qur'an Al-Azim, which is famous by the title Tafseer-e-Ibn-e-Kaseer, by Al-Hafiz.

It is one of the most famous Islamic books concerned with the science of interpretation of the Quran.

Author: Ismā‘īl ibn ‘Umar ibn Kathīr

Published in English 2006

17

Tafseer of the Quran must start with?

Correct Answer: All
Show explanation
Explanation

Tafsir bi-riwāyah connotes tafsir using another portion of the Quran, or sayings of Muhammad, or saying of his companions.

The word tafsīr is derived from the three-letter Arabic verbal root of ف-س-ر F-S-R (fassara, 'interpreted').

In its literal meaning, the word refers to interpreting, explaining, expounding, or disclosing

ND23-6-2023




18

The meaning of Kausar is:

Correct Answer: The Abundance
Show explanation
Explanation

The meaning of Surah Al Kausar is Abundance.

It is the 108th surah of the Quran and is one of the shortest surahs, with only three verses.

The surah was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) during the early days of his prophethood

19

What was the father's name of Hazrat Annas R.A?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Malik
Show explanation
Explanation

Anas ibn Mālik ibn Naḍr al-Khazrajī al-Anṣārī  was a well-known sahabi (companion) of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

Family: Banu Najjar

Born: c. 611 or 612 CE Medina, Hejaz

Died: c. 712 CE (93 AH) (Aged 100–103) Basra, Umayyad CaliphateArabia 

The shrine of Anas Ibn Malik is located in Basra, Iraq

Other names: ibn Malik

Parents: Malik ibn Nadr (father), Umm Sulaym (mother)

***
Known for:

Companion of the Prophet

The transmitter of Hadiths

The final companion to pass away, He was the last living companion of The Prophet ‎ﷺ

How many years Hazrat Muhammad spent in Makka after prophet hood ?

Correct Answer: 13

Explanation

The conquest of Mecca by Muslims led by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in December 629 or January 630 AD (Julian), 20 Ramadan, 8 AH.

The Messenger of Allah (S.A.W.) entered Makkah on his favorite camel Al-Kaswa

having Usama Ibn Zaid (R.A.) sitting behind him.

On his way, he recited Surah Al-Fatha (48th Surah of the Quran)

22

حضرت علی رَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ مسلمانوں کے ــــــــــــــــــــــــــــ خلیفہ ہیں

Correct Answer: چوتھے
Show explanation
Explanation

پہلے خلیفہ: حضرت ابوبکررَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ

تیسرے خلیفہ: حضرت عثمان رَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ

چوتھے خلیفہ: حضرت علی رَضِیَ اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَنْہُ

23

Hazrat Ayesha was Hazrat Muhammad which wife?

Correct Answer: third
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker & Wife Of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

Aisha was married to Muhammad in Mecca in 624 CE, after the Battle of Badr.
Resting place Jannat al-Baqi, Medina

Hazrat Ayesha Remained alive 46 years after the demise of Muhammad (PBUH).

Siddiqa was the title of Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths

"Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.

1.       HAZRAT KHADEJA,

2.       HAZRAT SAUDA

3.       HAZRAT AYESHA

4.       HAZRAT HAFSA

5.       HAZRAT ZAINAB KHUZEMA

6.       HAZRAT UMM E SALMA

7.       HAZRAT ZAINAB BINT E JAHESH

8.       HAZRAT JAVERIA

9.       HAZRAT UMM E HABIBA

10.   HAZRAT SAFIA

11.   HAZRAT MEMONA RA

25

The Prayer offered during a lunar eclipse is……?

Correct Answer: Namaz e Khusoof
Show explanation
Explanation

Prayer offered during a lunar eclipse is Namaz e Khusoof

and 

Namaz-e-Kasoof (نماز کسوف) is offered at the time of the solar eclipse. 

 ******

**********

  1. The lunar eclipse takes place when The shadow of the earth falls on the moon
  2. There are three types of lunar eclipses:

a total lunar eclipse,

a penumbral lunar eclipse

and a partial lunar eclipse.

***

ND4-12-2022


26

What is the meaning of Iman Mufassal:

Correct Answer: The belief in detail
Show explanation
Explanation

The meaning of Iman Mufassal: The belief in detail


Seven essential beliefs are present in Iman-e-Mafassal

1.  Faith in God.

2.  Faith in holy angels.

3.  Faith in all holy Books.

4.  Faith in all holy Prophets.

5.  Faith in the Day of Judgement.

6.  Faith in everything good or bad is decided by God (Taqdir).

7.  Faith in life after death.

Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

Hazrat Bilal called the first Azan of  Fajar prayer

Hazrat  Usman added the 2nd Azan for Friday prayers.


ND22-6-2023


32

If the current changes from 5A to nill in five seconds in a coil of 500 volts, then the self-inductance is _____ Henry?

Correct Answer: 500
Show explanation
Explanation

To find the self-inductance of a coil, we can use the formula:

L = (ΔΦ / ΔI)

Where:

L is the self-inductance in Henry (H),

ΔΦ is the change in magnetic flux in Weber (Wb),

and ΔI is the change in current in Ampere (A).

In this case, the current changes from 5A to 0A (nil) in 5 seconds. (ΔI) is therefore 5A - 0A = 5A.

To find the change in (ΔΦ), we can use Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction

ΔΦ = L * ΔI

Since the current changes from 5A to 0A in 5 seconds, the rate of  (ΔI) is 5A / 5s = 1A/s.

Substituting the values into the equation:

ΔΦ = L * ΔI

500 V = L * 1 A/s

Since the (V) is equal to  (L) and  (ΔI), we can rewrite the equation as:

L = 500 V / 1 A/s

Simplifying the equation gives us:

L = 500 H

Therefore, the self-inductance of the coil is 500 Henry (H).

33

39

Who gave the idea of Moat?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Salman Farsi
Show explanation
Explanation

Moat meaning کھائی

Hazrat Salman Al-Farisi gives the suggestion of digging the trench around the city of Madinah at the time of Battle of Khandaq.

Battle of Khandaq was a 27-day-long defense by Muslims of Yathrib from Arab and Jewish tribes.

Battle of Khandaq Start date: March 627 AD (5H)

40

Who introduced Islamization in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq
Show explanation
Explanation

General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq introduced Islamization in Pakistan.

Islamization has a long history in Pakistan since the 1950s,

but it became the primary policy, or "centerpiece" of the government of General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq,

the ruler of Pakistan from 1977 until his death in 1988.

41

The battle of Yamama was fought in the era of _____.

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakar (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

The Battle of Yamama was fought in December 632 

between the Rashidun Caliphate's forces of Abu Bakr and the Banu Hanifa's forces of Musaylima in the region of Yamama.

یہ جنگ جھوٹے نبی مسلمہ کے خلاف لڑی گئی

****

Resulting in the victory of the Rashidun Caliphate.

In the battle, 1,200 Muslims were killed,

with 360 of them being the memorizers of the Quran.

45

K2 is the second highest mountain in the world is located along the border of Pakistan and which country___?

Correct Answer: China
Show explanation
Explanation
  • 'K' standing for Karakoram.
  • Border: Pakistan (Gilgit-Baltistan) and China (Xinjiang).
  • Mountain Range: Karakoram Range.
  • Nicknames: Savage Mountain, Mount Godwin-Austen.
  • Second-highest mountain on Earth.
  • The world's highest peak, Mount Everest.
  • Height 8,611 meters

Last updated question NS on 09 MAY 2026


46

When did Sir Syed Ahmad set M.A.O High school in Aligarh?

Correct Answer: 1875
Show explanation
Explanation

Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College was founded in 1875 by Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, 


History
He was initially M.A.O as a primary school

after two years it became Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental College.

Lord Lytton inaugurated M.A.O College in 1877

In MAO Intermediate classes were started in 1878

in 1881 B.A. degree classes were added.

In 1887, it began to prepare students to enter Thomason College of Civil Engineering at Roorkee

47

Who compiled Sunan An-Nasai?

Correct Answer: Al Nisai
Show explanation
Explanation

Al-Sunan al-Sughra, also known as Sunan an-Nasa'i, is one of the Kutub al-Sittah, and was collected by Al-Nasa'i 

It contains roughly 5700 hadith (with repetitions) in 52 books.



48

Ethics are set of

Correct Answer: Moral standards

Explanation

Ethics are the set of moral principles that guide a person's behavior.

 Ethics reflect beliefs about what is right, what is wrong

49

Name the Mosque which was famous for the change of Qiblah?

Correct Answer: Masjid-e- Qiblatain
Show explanation
Explanation

Masjid e Qiblatain is a place where Prophet Muhammad (P.B.U.H)  change his direction (Qibla) from Masjid e Aqsa to Masjid ul Haraam during the Zohar namaz.

Masjid al-Qiblatain is one of the three oldest mosques in Islamic history

Masjid al-Qiblatain has 2 Dome's

Masjid al-Qiblatain has 2 Minare's

it was built by Abraham and Ishmael

Masjid al-Qiblatain first Opened: 1987

Now the Founder of Masjid al-Qiblatain is : Sawad ibn Ghanam ibn Ka'ab

ND5-2-2023

50

The first authentic Book of sahhaye sittah is___

Correct Answer: Sahih Bukhari
Show explanation
Explanation

ؒList of Siha-e-Sita Books

1. Sahih al Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari (d. 870), includes 7275 ahadith

2. Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim b. al-Hajjaj (d. 875), includes 9200 ahadith

3. Sunan Nasa’i, collected by an-Nasa'i (d. 915)

4. Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood (d. 888)

5. Jami at-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi (d. 892)

6. Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah (d. 887)

RK462023

51

Who is called Saqi-e-Zam Zam?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abbas (R.A)
Show explanation
Explanation

HAzrat Abbas(R.A)is called Saqi Zam Zam because he would quench the thirst of Pilgrims (Hijjis).
52

The six authentic books of hadiths are called

Correct Answer: Sihah Sitta
Show explanation
Explanation

ؒList of Siha-e-Sita Books

1. Sahih al Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari (d. 870), includes 7275 ahadith

2. Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim b. al-Hajjaj (d. 875), includes 9200 ahadith

3. Sunan Nasa’i, collected by an-Nasa'i (d. 915)

4. Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood (d. 888)

5. Jami at-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi (d. 892)

6. Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah (d. 887)

ؒList of Siha-e-Sita Books

1. Sahih al Bukhari, collected by Imam Bukhari (d. 870), includes 7275 ahadith

2. Sahih Muslim, collected by Muslim b. al-Hajjaj (d. 875), includes 9200 ahadith

3. Sunan Nasai, collected by an-Nasa'i (d. 915) includes 5270 ahadith

4. Sunan Abu Dawood, collected by Abu Dawood (d. 888)  includes 4800 ahadith

5. Jami at-Tirmidhi, collected by al-Tirmidhi (d. 892)  includes 3956 ahadith

6. Sunan ibn Majah, collected by Ibn Majah (d. 887)  includes 4000 ahadith

53

Arfat gathering is held on_____?

Correct Answer: 9th Zul-Haj
Show explanation
Explanation

Youm e Arfat is the second day of the Hajj pilgrimage and the day after is the first day of the major Islamic holiday of Eid al-Adha.

Held on 9th Zul-Haj 

54

What was the occupation of Hazrat Dawood alaihis salaam?

Najibullah  is the title of Hazrat Dawood (AS) 

The holy book Zaboor revealed to Hazrat Dawood (AS)

Hazrat Dawood (AS) is buried in Israel.

Hazrat Dawood is called caliph of Allah.

Hazrat Dawood (AS) is mentioned in the Holy Quran for 16 times.

Bani Israel unanimously acknowledged Hazrat Dawood A.S as their king



55

Which nation unanimously acknowledged Hazrat Dawood A.S as their king?

Correct Answer: Bani Israel
Show explanation
Explanation

Najibullah  is the title of Hazrat Dawood (AS) 

The holy book Zaboor revealed to Hazrat Dawood (AS)

Hazrat Dawood (AS) is buried in Israel.

Hazrat Dawood is called caliph of Allah.

Hazrat Dawood (AS) is mentioned in the Holy Quran for 16 times.

Bani Israel unanimously acknowledged Hazrat Dawood A.S as their king



56

The year of death of Tahzeeb ul Ahkam writer is:

57

How many times Hazrat Yunus is mentioned in the holy Quran?

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation

Surah Younis is the 10th chapter (surah) of the Quran with 109 verses (ayat).

Hazrat Yunus is named after the prophet Yunus (Jonah).

Hazrat Younis remained in the abdoman of fish for 40 days.

Hazrat Yunis is mentioned in the holy Quran: 4 times

Hazrat Younis (AS) is called Dhun-Nun 

 

58

The literal meaning of "Wahi" is ________?

Correct Answer: Allah's Message
Show explanation
Explanation

The literal meaning of "Wahi" is:  Allah's Message

2 kinds of Wahi are there

1 Wahee-e-Matlu:

It means the revelation which God has transmitted in His own words and speech

2 Wahee-e-Ghair Matlu:

It is the revelation that Allah projected in the heart of the Holy Prophet.

ND11-2-2023



59

Which angel came to Hazrat Muhammad (ﷺ) with Wahi?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Jibbrail (علیہ)
Show explanation
Explanation


Islamic tradition holds that during one of his visits to Mount Hira in the year 609 CE, the angel Gabriel appeared to him and commanded Muhammad to recite 5 verses of surah Alaq that would later be included in the Quran.
60

Who was the Last writer Kitab-e-wahi?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Abi bin Kaab(RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

کاتِبِ وَحی کے اردو معانی

پوشیدہ

اسم, مذکر

آنحضرت صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم پر جو وحی نازل ہوئی تھی 

حضورّ کے ارشاد کے مطابق لکھنے والا

Hazrat Sharjeel (R.A) is the first writer of Wahi and he wrote  Bismillah

**

ND16-1-2023

61

Katib e Quran is the attribute (Publisher of this holly book) of Hazrat_______?

Correct Answer: Usman (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Usman (R.A)

Belonged

Quraysh

Born

Makkah

Caliph

Third caliph

Caliphate

23 to 35 Hijjah

Caliphate

644 to 656 CE

Titles

Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

Martyred

18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

Martyrdom day

Friday

Wives

Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

Full Name

Usman bin Affaan

Death cause

Martyred by rebels

Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

 Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


62

Abu Talib was the ______ of Hazrat Muhammad SAW

Correct Answer: Uncle
Show explanation
Explanation

He was an uncle of the Islamic prophet Muhammad, and the father of Ali.

Abu Talib died around 619 AD, at more than 80 years of age, about 10 years after the start of Muhammad's mission (نبوت).

This year is known as the Year of Sorrow for Muhammad, because not only did his uncle Abu Talib die, but also his wife Khadijah bint Khuwaylid, within a month of Abu Talib.

After Abu Talib's death, Muhammad was left unprotected.




63

Hazrat Musa(A.S.) married the daughter of?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Shoaib A.S
Show explanation
Explanation

Bibi Safoora the wife of (Prophet Moosa) was the daughter of Prophet:  Hazrat Shoaib A.S

Hazrat Musa also known as Kaleemullah was one of 124,000 prophets of God.

Moses, also known as Moshe Rabbenu

Hazrat Musa was granted 9 miracles

Hazrat Musa (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the 6 heaven

***

ND29-1-2023


64

Hazrat Ali (R.A) was buried in which city?

Correct Answer: Najaf
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Ali (R.A) was attacked with a poisoned sword On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri.

Hazrat Ali (R.A) went a few moments before the time of Fajar prayers to the mosque of Kufa.

Hazrat Ali (R.A) started the prayers and when he went into the prostration (Sajda) Ibn-e-Muljim wounded him with his poisoned sword on his head.

The scar was on his head upto his forehead.

He died on the 21st Ramadan 40 A.H. he was  buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).

65

Which person is remembered as teacher of Madina ____?

Correct Answer: Musab Bin Umair
Show explanation
Explanation

Musab ibn Umair was a sahabi of Muhammad.

From the Banu Abd al-Dar branch of the Quraysh,

He embraced Islam in 614 CE and was the first ambassador of Islam.

He Died in the Battle of Uhud in 625 CE

66

The holy prophet established the first Islamic state in Medina in _____.

Correct Answer: 14th Nabvi
Show explanation
Explanation

After Muhammad ﷺ migrated from Mecca to Yathrib in 622 CE, he established the first Islamic state.

The holy prophet established the first Islamic state in Medina in approximately in 14th Nabvi or 1st Hijri

67

Last two surah of the Holy Quran are:

Correct Answer: Surah Al falaq & Surah An Nas
Show explanation
Explanation

Al Falaq is the 113th and An Nas is the 114 chapter of the Holy Quran.

There are 114 chapters in the Holy Quran.

68

Which Turkish leader abolished the Institution of Caliphate?

Correct Answer: Mustafa Kamal Attaturk
Show explanation
Explanation
It was further undermined when Mustafa Kemal Atatürk drove the Greeks from western Asia Minor in 1922 and deposed the Turkish sultan Mehmed VI in the same year.
The movement finally collapsed when Atatürk abolished the caliphate altogether in 1924.

69

Al Fateha, Al-Baqara, Al-e-Imran, Al Nisa are ______ Surahs.

Correct Answer: Madani
Show explanation
Explanation

28 مدنی سورتیں کتنی ہیں؟

The Medinan Surahs occur mostly at the beginning and in the middle of the Qur'an

The Medinan Surahs typically have more and longer ayat 

The Madni Surahs were revealed at Medina after Muhammad's hijra from Mecca.

Madani Surahs revealed in Year = 10

the first complete Madani surah of the Quran is Al-Baqarah

The total number of Surah in the Holy Quran is 114 the number of Makki Surah is 86 and Madni is 28

List of 28 Madani Surah in Quran:

1. Al Baqarah سورہ البقرہ

2. Al e Imran سورہ آل عمران

3. An Naas سورہ النسائ

4. Al Maaeda سورہ المائدہ

5. An Faal سورہ الانفال

6. Towba سورہ التوبہ

7. Al Raad سورہ الرعد

8. Al Haj سورہ الحج

9. Al Noor سورہ النور

10. Al Ahzab سورہ الاحزاب

11. Muhammad سورہ محمد

12. Al Fatah سورہ الفتح

13. Al Hujraat سورہ الحجرات

14. Al Rehman سورہ الرحمٰن

15. Al Hadeed سورہ الحدید

16. Al Mujadelah سورہ المجادلہ

17. Al Hashr سورہ الحشر

18. Al Mumtahina سورہ الممتحنہ

19. Al Saaf سورہ الصف

20. Al Juma سورہ الجمعہ

21. Al Munafiqon سورہ المنافقون

22. Al Tagabun سورہ التغابن

23. Al Talaq سورہ الطلاق

24. Al Tahreem سورہ التحریم

25. Al Insan سورہ الانسان

26. Al Baeana سورہ البینہ

27. Al Zalzalah سورہ الزلزال

28. Al Nasar سورہ النصر

Which OIC conference was held in Pakistan?

Correct Answer: 22–24 February 1974
Show explanation
Explanation
OIC role in Pakistan-Bangladesh relationship
It was under the pretext of the 2nd summit of OIC held in Lahore between 22 and 24 February 1974
This event is also known as the Lahore Summit.
It was a significant gathering where 36 member states participated

1st Extraordinary summit of OIC held in Islamabad between 23–24 March 1997
71

In Surah _______, every verse ends with letter ‘Dal’.

Correct Answer: Ikhlas
Show explanation
Explanation
Al-Ikhlāṣ, also known as the Declaration of God's Unity and al-Tawhid, is the 112th chapter of the Quran.
It is equal in value to a third part of the whole Quran.

Correct Answer: 656 hijri
Show explanation
Explanation

The Abbasid dynasty or Abbasids were an Arab dynasty that ruled the Abbasid Caliphate between 750 and 1258.

They were from the Qurayshi Hashimid clan of Banu Abbas, descended from Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib.

 Abbasid dynasty by the Mongols under Hulagu Khan

Al-Mustaʿṣim, (born 1212—died 1258), the last ʿAbbāsid caliph in Baghdad

The first Abbasid caliph, Abu al-ʿAbbas al-Saffah,

Nimrod, the king of Babylon, commanded his men to throw Prophet Ibrahim (AS) into a huge pit of burning fire

Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days

Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq

Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.

Abraham had two children,

1.      Isaac

2.      Ismael,

Tawaf Al-Qudum is_____?

75

For how long did the Prophet Muhammad and his companion remain in the siege in Shi’ab-e-Abi-Talib?

Correct Answer: 3 years
Show explanation
Explanation

Banu Hashim and Banu Al-Muttalib, who followed suit, were thus confined within a narrow pass (referred to as 'Shi'b of Abu Talib')

from the beginning of Muharram,

the seventh year of Muhammad's mission till the tenth year,

which is a period of three years.

Hajr-e-Aswad means:

Correct Answer: Black stone

Hajr-e-Aswad is a rock set into the eastern corner of the Kaaba, the ancient building in the center of the Grand Mosque in Makkah.


77

Which is the ninth month of Islamic Calendar?

Correct Answer: Ramzan
Show explanation
Explanation

Fast was obligatory in 2nd Hijri.
Muslim keep fast in month of Ramzan
Ramadan is divided into three stages-first Ashra (Rehmat), second Ashra (Maghfirat) and the third Ashra (Nijaat).

******

Must remember all 12 Islamic months name

List of Islamic Months

  1. Muḥarram, محرم الحرام
  2. Ṣafar, صفر المظفر
  3. Rabi al Awwal ربیع الاول
  4. Rabi al Sani ربیع الثانی
  5. Jumada al Awwal جمادی الاول
  6. Jumada al Thani جمادی الثانی
  7. Rajab, رجب المرجب
  8. Shabanشعبان المعظم
  9. Ramadan(fasting), رمضان المبارک
  10. Shawwal شوال المکرم
  11. Dhu al Qadah ذوالقعدۃ الحرام
  12. Dhu al Hijjah ذوالحجۃ الحرام:

Each month begins approximately at the time of the new moon.

82

How many Quraysh people captured in the Battle of Badr?

Correct Answer: 70
Show explanation
Explanation

All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

 mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

Total: Muslim 313-317

Muhajirun: 82-85

Ansar: 231

Aws: 61

Khazraj: 170

2 horses

70 camels

**

while

Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin translate the Holy Quran into the Urdu language in the first time in 1826

Shah Waliullah was the father of Shah Abdul Qadir.

while 

The Holy Quran was first time translated into Urdu:

Shah Abdul Qadir and Shah Rafiuddin (Mozah Al-Quran)

The Holy Quran was first time translated into Punjabi:

Tauqir Amaan Khan

The Holy Quran was first time translated into Sindhi:

Aakhund Azzizullah

The Holy Quran first time translated into English:

George Sale

Holy Quran was translated for the first time in India

Sindhi

Holy Quran was translated for the first time in World

Persian

92

Which Surah of Holy Quran is about Orphans?

Correct Answer: Surah al Nisa
Show explanation
Explanation

بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ

يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُواْ رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ وَخَلَقَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا وَبَثَّ مِنْهُمَا رِجَالاً كَثِيراً وَنِسَاء وَاتَّقُواْ اللّهَ الَّذِي تَسَاءلُونَ بِهِ وَالأَرْحَامَ إِنَّ اللّهَ كَانَ عَلَيْكُمْ رَقِيبًا


In the Name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful

1."O' people! be in awe of your Lord, Who created you from a single soul and from it created its mate and spread from them (the couple) many men and women. And be in awe of Allah through Whom you demand one of another, as well as any ties of kinship; Surely Allah is watching over you."

93

Which companion of Holy Prophet (SAAW) recite verse of Holy Quran at the presence of Najjashi?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Jaffar Tyar
Show explanation
Explanation

Ja'far then recited a passage from Surah Maryam. When the Najashi heard it, he wept and exclaimed: Verily, this (Quran) and what Isa brought (Gospel) has come from the same source of light.


94

______is the person who performs prayer alone.

Correct Answer: Munfarid
Show explanation
Explanation

Munfarid is the person who performs prayer alone.
Madurak is the person who starts prayer with Immam
Musbaq is the person who comes after one rakat

95

Which of the following Surah is revealed in respect of Ahle Bayt?

Correct Answer: Ahzab
Show explanation
Explanation

There is nothing about Ahle bait in surah Dahr

but in SURA AHZAB ALAH says o wives of prophet you are not like any other women

مختلف ویب سائیٹ میں یہ سوال غلط دیا ہوا ہے اس کا صیح جواب سورہ احزاب ہے



Last updated question by NA on 07 May 2026

96

Where is the grave of Hazrat Aisha (R.A.)?

Correct Answer: Jannat al-Baqi
Show explanation
Explanation

Aisha was buried at Jannat al-Baqi.


Aisha died at her home in Medina on 17 Ramadan 58 AH (16 July 678).

She was 67 years old.

Abu Hurairah led her funeral prayer after the tahajjud (night) prayer

Hazrat Aisha (R.A) was 18 years old when Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) died 

Hazrat Aisha (RA) lived 45 years after the death of Prophet Muhammad (SAW)


Additional Information

Hazrat Ayesha was the 3rd wife and most learned of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths

Muhammad married Hazrat Ayesha when he was 53 years old and she was only six years old.

He had intercourse with Hazrat Ayesha when she was nine years old.

Hazrat Ayesha was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker 

She gives the title of Siddiqa, meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man


97

Duration of Tofaan e Noah was______?

Correct Answer: Six months
Show explanation
Explanation

The duration of Tofaan e Noah was Six months

Boat of Hazrat Noah (A.S) is called Arc

Hazrat Nuh (AS) remained in the boat for about six months.

Boat of Noah stops in Turkey/ Jabl-e-Jodi

After Toofan-e-Noah, Khasran city was established.

About 80 people were with him in the boat.

After the 2242 years of Hazrat Adam A.S., the event of Toofan-e-Noah occurred.

ND11-2-2023

Correct Answer: Turkey

Boat of Hazrat Noah (A.S) is called Arc

Hazrat Nuh (AS) remained in the boat for about six months.

Boat of Noah stops in Turkey/ Jabl-e-Jodi

After Toofan-e-Noah, Khasran city was established.

99

The prophet whose nation was the last one to suffer the divine punishment was?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Saleh (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation
"Hazrat Saleh (A.S)" is a prophet mentioned in the Quran who prophesied to the tribe of Thamud in ancient Arabia, before the lifetime of Prophet Muhammad.

"Hazrat Saleh (A.S)" was chosen for prophethood at the age of 16

"Hazrat Saleh (A.S)"  Died: Mecca, Saudi Arabia

The Resting Place of "Hazrat Saleh (A.S)": Hasik (present-day Oman)

Prophet Saleh was a descendant of the great prophet Nuh

100

The Six Books of Hadith are known as ___________?

Correct Answer: Siha e Sitta
Show explanation
Explanation

Following are the six most famous books of Hadith:

 

1. Sahih Bukhari

Name: Muhammad ibn Ismail al-Bukhari

 

2. Sahih Muslim

Name:Abul Hassan Muslim AL Hajjaj

 

3. Jame Tirmizi

Name: Abu Esa Muhammad AL Trimidhi

 

4. Sunan Abu Dawood

Name:Abu Daud Suleiman bin Ash’ath

 

5. Sunan Al Sughra

Name: Abu adul Rahman al-Nasai

 

6. Sunan ibn Maja

Name: Abu Abdullah Mohammad ibn Yazid

What is age of Hazrat Yousaf (AS), when he was thrown into a well by his brothers_?

102

The Namaz e Janaza is ___

Correct Answer: Farz e Kaffaya
Show explanation
Explanation

No Sajdas are in Salat-e-Janaza

Namaz-e-Janazah is performed by standing in line

4 Takbeers are in Namaz-e-Janaza 

Namaz e Janaza is Farz e Kuffiya

The first Namaz-e-Janazah performed by Holy Prophet ﷺ was pf Asad bin Zaraara Sahabi

103

How many Sunnah are in four obligations of Ablution (Wazu)?

Correct Answer: 13
Show explanation
Explanation

Wazu (Ablution) was ordered on  5 A.H

The acts which break the ablution are called Nawaqiz-e-Wazu

There are 6 Sunnah acts of ablution

There are 4 obligations for ablution.

13 Sunnah are in four obligations of Ablution

By Hazrat Jibrail (علیہ), Prophet ﷺ learnt Salat & ablution

وضو کے چار فرائض ہیں۔

نمبر 1: چہرہ دھونا

نمبر 2: دونوں ہاتھوں کو کہینیوں تک دھونا

نمبر 3: چوتھائی سر کا مسح کرنا

نمبر 4:پاوں کا ٹخنیوں سمیت دھونا

****

    108

    The pilgrims throw pebbles at Satans. What is the name of this act:

    Correct Answer: Rami
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The pilgrims throw pebbles at Satans. the name of this act: Rami
    • ***
    • The Stoning of the Devil is part of the annual Islamic Hajj pilgrimage to the holy city of Mecca in Saudi Arabia.
    • During Hajj, pebbles are collected from where Muzdalfa
    • at least 49 pebbles are needed for the ritual.
    • Jamart throwing of pebbles is performed on the 10th, 11th, 12th, and 13th of Zul Hajj.
    • ***

    • Seven stones are thrown at the three pillars to commemorate Ibrahim's rejection of Satan.

    • The final ritual of the Hajj, the “Great Jamara”

    Pashto

    Sindhi

    and Balochi.

    while 

    Official Language of Pakistan: English, Urdu

    while

    Punjabi

     Pashto

    Sindhi

    Saraiki

    Urdu.

    while

    The most widely spoken language in Pakistan is Punjabi (48 % speak), followed by the Punjabi variant Siraiki (10 percent),

    Balochi 3%

    Ameer Muawiyah (R.A) established which system?

    Mu'awiya I  was the founder and first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate serving from 661 until his death.

     He became caliph less than thirty years after the death of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

    Muawiyah was appointed as the Governor of Syria after his brother Yazid ibn Abu Sufyan died

    Ameer Muawiyah (R.A) established Postal System

    Hazrat Abu Sufyan, Hazrat Ameer Muawiyah, and Abu Kahafa (R.A) accepted Islam at the time of the Conquest of Makkah

     these trenches were dug in 6 days

    Battle of the Ditch, (ad 627), Arabic Al-Khandaq (The Ditch), an early Muslim victory

    The battle is also referred to as the Battle of Confederates (غزوة الاحزاب).

    The Qur'an uses the term confederates (الاحزاب) in Surah Al-Ahzab 33:9-32

    Ghazwa e Khandaq was the 30-day-long fight of Yathrib by Arabian and Jewish tribes.

      135

      Third Ashra of Ramadan is known as ____?

      Correct Answer: Days to seek refuge from Hellfire
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The first Ashra is from the 1st to the 10th of Ramadan. 

       "First Ashra of Ramadan is "Days of mercy" (Rehmat),

      ***

      The mid-10 days are called the days of forgiveness (Maghfirat). 

      These days are called the second Ashra of Ramadan

      ***

      the last 30 days are called the third Ashra. 

      3rd is "days of refuge from hell fire"(Jahanum ke aag sa nijahat) "

      ND27122022



      136

      On the suggestion of whom, Holy Prophet (P.B.U.H) released the Prisoners of Ghazwa-e-Badr?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The name Badr means Moon That Is Full and is of Arabic origin.

      Battle of, Islam, in which Muhammad with about 300 men overcame a force of about 1000 Meccans.


      137

      When ‘Eid ul Adha’ is Celebrated?

      Correct Answer: Tenth day of the last month of the Islamic Lunar Calendar - Zil Hajj
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Eid al-Azha is celebrated annually on the 10th of Dhul-Hijjah

      Eid al-Azha in Arabic means the 'festival of the sacrifice'

      It marks the completion of Hajj

      Commemorates Prophet Ibrahim's (Abraham's) willingness to sacrifice his son in obedience to Allah's command.


      Additional information 

      Eid means a “festival” or “feast”

      Doing ghusl on Eid is mustahabb. 

      Eid Prayer is Wajib

      Eid Salat consists of two 'Rakats' and six 'Takbirs'.

      There are two Eid prayers in a year, 

      1. Eid-ul-Fitr 
      2. Eid-ul-adha.
      138

      When is Eid ul Fitr celebrated?

      Correct Answer: the first day of the month of Shawwal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Eid al-Fitr also called the "Festival of Breaking the Fast"

      Eid al-Fitr marks the end of Ramadan,

      and is celebrated during the first three days of Shawwal,

      Shawal is the 10th month of the Islamic calendar.

      Eid ul Fitr first performed on 2 AH

      ***




      139

      Hazrat Ali ibn Abu Talib (R. W) is buried at?

      Correct Answer: Najaf
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Ali ibn Abu Talib (R. W) is buried in najaf

      ***

      Ali was eventually killed in the mosque of Kufa by the sword of one of the Kharijites, Ibn Muljam Moradi, and was buried outside the city of Kufa

      Correct Answer: Belief in seven things.

      Explanation

      141

      What are Hanfi, Malki, Shafi and Hanbali ?

      Correct Answer: Fiqhi doctrines
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sunni Islam is divided into four schools of law or fiqh (religious jurisprudence):

      Hanafi

      Shafi

      Maliki

      and Hanbali

      ***

      Jibrael came _____ times into the court of the Holy Prophet?

      Correct Answer: 24,000

      146

      Who was the eldest daughter of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)?

      Correct Answer: Hazrat Zainab (R.A)
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Hazrat Zainab was the first and youngest daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah
      She married her maternal cousin, Abu al-As ibn al-Rabi'
      She had two children "Ali and Umamah"

      She died at the age of  30 (AH 7)and was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.



      Additional Information:


      Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
      1. Hazrat Qasim 
      2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
      3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
      4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
      5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
      6. Hazrat Abdullah
      7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

      All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

      151

      What Is the meaning of Tawheed?

      Correct Answer: Oneness
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Tawheed means the Oneness of Allah. No one likes worship without Allah.

      ***

      there are five pillars of Islam Tawheed is the first pillar 

      ***

      The Five Pillars of Islam

      The profession of Faith (shahada) Tawheed. The belief that "There is no god but God, and Muhammad is the Messenger of God" is central to Islam. 

      Prayer (salat). Muslims pray facing Mecca five times a day: at dawn, noon, mid-afternoon, sunset, and after dark

      Alms (zakat). 

      Fasting (sawm).

      Pilgrimage (hajj)

      156

      1st revelation on Muhammad was made in which cave ?

      Correct Answer: Hira
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The first revelation of the Holy Quran consisted of 5 Aayats.
      • **
      • Related questions about the revelation of the Holy Quran:
      • 2 years and six months was the interval between the first and the second revelation of the Holy Quran
      • 1st revelation of Muhammad was made in Hira. On 17 Ramzan 610 AD 
      • 23 years did it take for the complete revelation of the Quran
      • Prophet (SAW) used to meditate on Mount Hira before revelation.
      • According to Islamic Calendar, on the 21st of Ramadan the prophet Muhammad(PBUH) received the first revelations of the Quran
      • **
      • The Quran was verbally revealed from God to Muhammad through the angel Gabriel beginning on 22 December 609 CE,
      • when Muhammad was 40 and concluding in 632 CE, the year of his death.

      161

      What is the meaning of Khums?

      Correct Answer: One fifth
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • one fifth is the meaning of Khums
      • **
      • In Islam, KHUMS خمس refers to the required religious obligation of any Muslim to pay one-fifth of their acquired wealth from certain sources toward specified causes.
      • Khums is different and separate from other Islamic taxes such as zakat and jizya.
      • **
      • Usher and Khums are Two kinds of Tax in Islam,

      169

      The voltage across the 100W conductor is 250V. The current is _____?

      Correct Answer: 2.5 A
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      To find the current flowing through the conductor, you can use Ohm's Law, which states:

      Voltage (V)=Current (I)×Resistance (R)Voltage (V)=Current (I)×Resistance (R)

      Given that the power (P) of the conductor is 100 W100W, and the voltage (V) is 250 V250V, you can use the formula:

      Power (P)=Voltage (V)×Current (I)Power (P)=Voltage (V)×Current (I)

      Solve for current (I):

      Current (I)=Power (P)Voltage (V)Current (I)=Voltage (V)Power (P)​

      Substitute the given values:

      Current (I)=100 W250 V=0.4 ACurrent (I)=250V100W​=0.4A

      So, the current flowing through the conductor is 0.4 A0.4A.

      ایک ہی وقت میں پوری ملت اسلامیہ کا ایک عبادت میں مصروف رہنا، باہمی یگانگت کے فروغ کا سبب بنتا ہے۔

      اس اعتبار سے آپ صَلَّی اللہُ تَعَالٰی عَلَیْہِ وَاٰلِہٖ وَسَلَّم نے ماہ رمضان کو مواخات اور غمگساری کا مہینہ قراردیا ہے۔

      179

      If a person offers a prayer before its time with the previous prayer i.e. Offering of Asr prayer with Zuhar Prayer, then this salat is called.

      Correct Answer: Jama Taqdeem
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • if a person offers a prayer before its time with the previous prayer i.e. Offering of Asr prayer with Zuhar Prayer, then this salat is called Jama Taqdeem
      • ***
      • Jama' tanqdem means combining two prayers and they are performed in the first prayer time; that is for Zohor and Asar prayers performed in Zohor time; Maghrib and Isya' prayers performed in Maghrib time.

      183

      The Battle of Badr was fought on Ramadan 27, AH, Accordingly in:

      Correct Answer: 624 AD
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      All important questions and information related to battle of Badr

      Badr means in Arabic: Full moon

      Furqan the second name of the battle of Badr

      Abu Jehal was the leader of the kufars in the battle of Badar.

      The battle of Badr was the first battle of Islam 

      70 infidels were killed in Ghazwa Badr

      The Battle of Badr is also referred to as The Day of the Criterion.

      It was fought on Tuesday, 13 March 624 CE (17 Ramadan, 2 AH), near the city of Badr.

      14 Muslims were killed in the Battle of Badr.

      No of the Kufars (Non-Muslims Forces) in the Battle of Badr were 1000

       mentioned in Surat Al-Anfal 

      1000 angels participated in the Battle of Badr

      Total: Muslim 313-317

      Muhajirun: 82-85

      Ansar: 231

      Aws: 61

      Khazraj: 170

      2 horses

      70 camels

      **

      184

      Hazrat Ayesha Remained alive ______ years after the death of Muhammad (PBUH).

      Correct Answer: 46
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker & Wife Of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)

      Aisha was married to Muhammad in Mecca in 624 CE, after the Battle of Badr.
      Resting       place Jannat al-Baqi, Medina

      Hazrat Ayesha Remained alive 46 years after the demise of Muhammad (PBUH).

      Siddiqa was the title of Hazrat Ayesha (رضہ) meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

      Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths


      "Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.

      1.       HAZRAT KHADEJA,

      2.       HAZRAT SAUDA

      3.       HAZRAT AYESHA

      4.       HAZRAT HAFSA

      5.       HAZRAT ZAINAB KHUZEMA

      6.       HAZRAT UMM E SALMA

      7.       HAZRAT ZAINAB BINT E JAHESH

      8.       HAZRAT JAVERIA

      9.       HAZRAT UMM E HABIBA

      10.   HAZRAT SAFIA

      11.   HAZRAT MEMONA RA

      185

      Hajj is a pilgrimage of:

      Correct Answer: Holy Kaaba
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Hajj is a pilgrimage made to the Kaaba, the "House of Allah"

      It is one of the Five Pillars of Islam

      Hajj is a sacred pilgrimage that is required of every Muslim at least once in their lifetimes

      verbal meaning of Hajj is The will to visit

      There are 3 Farz, 10 Sunnat and 6 Wajibat in Hajj

        Farz in Hajj are Three (3)

        Farz in Umrah are Four (4)

        There are three different types of Hajj namely:
        Hajj-ul-Ifrad.
        Hajj-ul-Qiran.
        Hajj-ul-Tamattu.


        ND29-5-2023

        186

        Verbal meaning of Hajj ___?

        Correct Answer: The will to visit
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hajj is a pilgrimage made to the Kaaba, the "House of Allah"

        It is one of the Five Pillars of Islam

        Hajj is a sacred pilgrimage that is required of every Muslim at least once in their lifetimes

        verbal meaning of Hajj is The will to visit

        There are 3 Farz, 10 Sunnat and 6 Wajibat in Hajj

          Farz in Hajj are Three (3)

          Farz in Umrah are Four (4)

          187

          Battle of Badr was the first major encounter between the Muslims and the Mushriqeen-e-Makkah. Hazrat Usman (RA) could not take part in that battle because?

          Correct Answer: His wife Hazrat Ruqayya (RA) was sick
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Belonged

          Quraysh

          Born

          Makkah

          Caliph

          Third caliph

          Caliphate

          23 to 35 Hijjah

          Caliphate

          644 to 656 CE

          Titles

          Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

          Martyred

          18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

          Martyrdom day

          Friday

          Wives

          Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

          Full Name

          Usman bin Affaan

          Death cause

          Martyred by rebels

          Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran.

          Collection of the holy Quran was completed in?

          Correct Answer: 2 terms
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Collection of the holy Quran was completed in two terms:

          1. Hazrat Abubakar (RA)

          2. Hazrat Usman (RA)

          Out of the copies of the Quran compiled by Usmanؓ , how many are present?

          Remember Tips

          یاد کرنے کے لیے

          1. Rashidin -30 years

          193

          When was Meesaq-e-Madina signed?

          Correct Answer: 622 AD
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Constitution of Medina/Charter of Madina میثاق مدینہ 

          Signed: First Hijri/622 AD 

          Between: Muslims, Jews & other non-muslims 

          Clauses: 47

          Note: The Medina Charter consists of 47 clauses. 23 clause governed the relationship between Muslims, between Ansar and Muhajirin, while the remaining 24 clauses governed the relations of Muslims with non-Muslims, the Jews and others.


          194

          Charter of Medina consists of 47 clauses. How many clauses related to governed the relations of Muslims with non-Muslims?

          Correct Answer: 24
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Constitution of Medina/Charter of Madina میثاق مدینہ 

          Signed: First Hijri/622 AD 

          Between: Muslims, Jews & other non-muslims 

          Clauses: 47

          Note: The Medina Charter consists of 47 clauses. 23 clause governed the relationship between Muslims, between Ansar and Muhajirin, while the remaining 24 clauses governed the relations of Muslims with non-Muslims, the Jews and others.


          195

          Between whom the Charter of Medina was concluded?

          Correct Answer: Muslims and Jews and Other non-Muslims
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Constitution of Medina/Charter of Madina میثاق مدینہ 

          Signed: First Hijri/622 AD 

          Between: Muslims, Jews & other non-muslims 

          Clauses: 47

          Note: The Medina Charter consists of 47 clauses. 23 clause governed the relationship between Muslims, between Ansar and Muhajirin, while the remaining 24 clauses governed the relations of Muslims with non-Muslims, the Jews and others.


          196

          Meesaq-e-Madina was signed between?

          Correct Answer: Muslims & Jews
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Constitution of Medina/Charter of Madina میثاق مدینہ 

          Signed: First Hijri/622 AD 

          Between: Muslims, Jews & other non-muslims 

          Clauses: 47

          Note: The Medina Charter consists of 47 clauses. 23 clause governed the relationship between Muslims, between Ansar and Muhajirin, while the remaining 24 clauses governed the relations of Muslims with non-Muslims, the Jews and others.


          197

          Ethics is a system of______?

          Correct Answer: Moral/Principles
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Ethics is a normative science. 

          It is concerned with what ought to be done rather than what is the case.

          It differs from positive science.

          A positive science, natural science or descriptive science is concerned with what is.

          It deals with facts and explains them by their causes.

          198

          Hazrat Umar accepted Islam in ___ AD.

          Correct Answer: 616
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          At the time he embraced Islam, Hazrat Umar R.A. was about 32 years old.

          He was born in 584 AD.

          Hazrat Umar R.A embraced Islam in 616 AD


          MUG 10-12-2022


          199

          What is the meaning “Zam Zam"?

          Correct Answer: To Stop
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Zamzam water is colourless and odorless, but has a distinct taste, with a pH of 7.5–7.7, and so is slightly alkaline.

          200

          What is the fundamental pillar of Islam which requires both physical and financial sacrifices ?

          Correct Answer: Hajj
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hajj made compulsory in 09 A.H.

          First Hajj offered in 9 A.H

          The Holy Prophet Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ performed only 1 Hajj in 10 A.H.

          Number of Jamarat in hajj is three.
          201

          Treaty of Hudaybiyyah took place in which Hijri?

          Correct Answer: 6th
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (R.A).

          The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was an event that took place during the time of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

          It was a pivotal treaty between Muhammad, representing the state of Medina, and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca 

          Sulah e Hudaibiya is called Fatah Al-Mubeen in Surah Al-Fatah

          Sulah e Hudaibiya Was Signed In The Year 628/ 6H.

          Sule e Huabaibiya Was Signed For 10 Years.

          The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah took place in March 628 Dhu al-Qi’dah

          7 clauses did the treaty of Hudaybiyyah contain

          Hudabia is the name of the tree.

          Hazrat Muhammadﷺ, representing the state of Medina

          Suhail bin Amar signed the Treaty of Hudaibiya on behalf of the Quraish Mecca.


          202

          What is the arrangement number of Surah Al-Nas?

          Correct Answer: 114
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Al-Nās or Mankind is the 114th and last chapter of the Qur'an.

          It is a short six-verse invocation

          Classification: Meccan

          No. of letters: 80

          No. of verses: 6

          No. of words: 20

          Other names: The Men, People

          The Holy Prophet (PBUH) was born on__?

          Mother : Amina

          Father name Abdullah

          Family: Quraish

          Born in Makkah

          Died in 632 AD

          Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

          "Hazrat Muhammad PBUH Had 11th Wives.

          204

          Imam Bukhari was born in:

          Correct Answer: 20 July 810 AD
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Imām Bukhāri was a 9th-century Muslim muhaddith.

          Imam Bukhari is widely regarded as the most important hadith scholar in the history of Islam.

          Imam Bukhari was born in 194 AH.

          Imam Bukhari died in 256 AH.

          He belongs to Uzbekistan.

          Buried: September 1, 870 AD, Tomb Of Imam Bukhari, Xoʻja Ismoil, Uzbekistan

          Real name: Muhammad bin Ismaeel

          205

          Which surah of Holy Quran is known as Surah Widah?

          Correct Answer: Surah e Al Nasr
          Show explanation
          Explanation

           

          Surah Al Nasr called Surah Widah because revealed in the last.

          Al Nasr is 110th surah in the Quran has 3 verses. It is in the para or juz 30 of the Quran.

           

          Al Nasr is the third-shortest surah after Al-Asr and Al-Kawthar by number of ayat.

          Surah Al nasr is 4rth shortest surah of quran aftrer surah ikhlas, surah asr and surah kawthar.

          بِسْمِ اللّٰهِ الرَّحْمٰنِ الرَّحِیْمِ قُلْ اَعُوْذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ(1) مَلِكِ النَّاسِ(2) اِلٰهِ النَّاسِ(3) مِنْ شَرِّ الْوَسْوَاسِ ﳔ الْخَنَّاسِ(4) الَّذِیْ یُوَسْوِسُ فِیْ صُدُوْرِ النَّاسِ(5) مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَ النَّاسِ(6).

          206

          the literal meaning of zulnorain is----?

          Correct Answer: Having two Lights
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • the literal meaning of zulnorain is Having two Lights
          • ***
          • zulnorain titles of Hazrat Usman, the third Rightly Guided Caliph,
          • who married two of Prophet Muhammad's daughters, viz. Hazrat Ruqaiyya and Hazrat Umm-e-Kulsoom
          • ***
          • Zulnorain is the title of Usman (R.A).
          • This title was given by Khadija (R.A).

          which of the follwoing is included among the Ashra-e-Mubashera?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Saad Bin Abi Waqas(RA)

          208

          First complete Madani Surah is?

          Correct Answer: Al-Baqarah
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          The first complete Madani surah of the Quran is Al-Baqarah
          Al-Baqarah is the second and longest chapter of the Quran.
          Position: Juz 1–3
          It consists of:
          • 286 verses
          • 6,201 words
          • 25,500 letters
          It has been so named from the story of the Cow (vv. 67-73).
          282 verse  is the longest verse of this surah is known as ayatu d-dayn
          13 times Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ) is mentioned in Sura Baqarah
          183 Ayat of Surah Al-Baqarah describes the obligation of soam

          Omar bin Abdul Aziz was the governor of:

          Correct Answer: Madina

          Died 5 February 720 (aged 37)

          210

          Who was the mother of Hazrat Ishaq (RA)?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Sara
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Sara (RA) was the wife of Hazrat Ibraheem (AS) and the mother of Hazrat Ishaq (AS), sister Hazrat Loot (RA)

          Hazrat Ishaq (RA) was the father of Hazrat Yaqoob (AS).

          211

          Representative of Hazrat Imam Hussain (RA) to Kufa was:

          Correct Answer: Muslim bin Aqeel
          Show explanation

          Muslim bin Aqeel was the son of Aqil ibn Abi Talib.

          He was a cousin of Imam Hussain (RA).

           

          212

          What is the meaning of Qibla?

          Correct Answer: Something in front
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Qibla is the direction towards the Kaaba in the Sacred Mosque in Makkah.

          213

          Jabal Malaikah is also known as:

          Correct Answer: Mountain of Angels
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Jabal Malaikah is a mountain located beside the battlefield of Badr. It is from this mountain that the angels came to assist the Prophet (PBUH) and the Sahabah during the Battle of Badr. 500 angels came to the right side of the army, led by Hazrat Jibraeel

          214

          Who compiled Sunan Ibn Majah?

          Correct Answer: Ibn Majah.
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Author Ibn Mājah

          Language Arabic

          Series Kutub al-Sittah

          Genre Hadith collection

          *******

          VU FIA PAPER 18-12-2021

          215

          The Amount of Zakat cannot be used in ______.

          Correct Answer: Mosque
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Zakat is not permissible to be spent on the construction of a mosque.
          • This is because it does not fit under any of the eight categories
          • List of Head of Zakat

          1. Al-Fuqara (The poor)

          2. Al-Masakin (The needy)

          3. Zakat collectors

          4. Who converted to Islam

          5. Freeing captives

          6. Debtors

          7. In the path of Allah

          8. Wayfarer (Traveler)

          ...............

          • Zakat became compulsory during the 2nd hijrah but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.
          • The word Zakat occurs in the Holy Quran 32 Times
          • The word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs 22 times
          • Parents, Husband, wife and children are not entitled to get Zakat

          216

          After the death of ___________, Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Correct Answer: Ruqayyah (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Belonged

          Quraysh

          Born

          Makkah

          Caliph

          Third caliph

          Caliphate

          23 to 35 Hijjah

          Caliphate

          644 to 656 CE

          Titles

          Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

          Martyred

          18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

          Martyrdom day

          Friday

          Wives

          Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

          Full Name

          Usman bin Affaan

          Death cause

          Martyred by rebels

          Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


          217

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with____________

          Correct Answer: Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Belonged

          Quraysh

          Born

          Makkah

          Caliph

          Third caliph

          Titles

          Martyrdom day

          Friday

          Wives

          Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

          Full Name

          Usman bin Affaan

          Death cause

          Martyred by rebels

          Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


          218

          The holy prophet (PBUH) took part in ______________ Battles.

          Correct Answer: 27
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Battle means Ghazwa.

          Twenty-seven (27) battles out of the twenty-nine (29) battles were fought with the attendance of the Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) himself.

           

          219

          Muslims fought their first Naval Battle in 655 AD during the reign of ___ .

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman
          Show explanation
          Explanation


          220

          Muslims fought their first Naval Battle against ___

          Correct Answer: Byzantines
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Battle of the Masts or Battle of Phoenix was a crucial naval battle fought in 655 between the Muslim Arabs led by Abu al-A'war and the Byzantine fleet under the personal command of Emperor Constans II.

          The battle was part of the earliest campaign by Muawiyah to reach Constantinople and is considered to be "the first decisive conflict of Islam on the deep".


          221

          Which one is the significant event of 6th Hijrah?

          Correct Answer: Treaty of Hudabia
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The incident of Hudaibiyah reserves in history a significant phase of Islam when Muslims got an opportunity to unexpectedly notch gains from the jaws of an apparent defeat.

          It was the sixth year of Hijrah since the Prophet (peace be upon him) and his supporters had left the beloved city of Makkah.

          222

          Abdullah bin Salam (R.S) was a convert from __________ to Islam.

          Correct Answer: Judaism
          Show explanation
          Explanation

           Abdullah ibn Salam was a Jew who converted to Islam.

          He was a Jew in Yathrib who belonged to the Banu Qaynuqa tribe 

          He was a companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

          He participated in the conquest of Syria and Palestine, 
          He died in Medina.

          223

          Shifa bint Abdullah (R.S) was employed by Umar Bin Khattab ( R.S) as _________?

          Correct Answer: inspector of market
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Shifa bint Abdullah (R.S) was employed by Umar Bin Khattab ( R.S) as inspector of market

          His given name was Layla,

          Al-Shifaa became a Muslim in Mecca

          There she had a house between the mosque and the market.

           Al-Shifaa was the first woman to acquire read-write

          224

          Who was called Ammen-ul-Umat

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Ubidah Bin Jaraah (ra)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Amir Ibn Abdullah Ibn Jarrah was a Muslim commander 

          Hazrat Amir Ibn Abdullah Ibn Jarrah was called Ammen-ul-Umat

          Born: 583 Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia

          Died: 639 (aged 55–56) Jordan Valley (Middle East)

          Burial place: Ammata, Jordan

          Known For:

          Being a companion of Muhammad

          one of the ten to whom Paradise was promised

          225

          Companions of Prophet PBUH at Bait-e-Shajars were:

          Correct Answer: 1400
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          بیعة الشجرة یا بیعة الرضوان ایک عہد تھا

          جو اسلامی پیغمبر محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم سے ان کے صحابہ (صحابہ کرام) نے لیا تھا۔

          Bait e Rizwan/Pledge of the TreeIn was made 628/6 AH.

          Bait-e-Rizwan took place in 6th Hijrah.

          Bait Rizwan was the pledge taken to get revenge on Hazrat Usman (RA) under the tree known as Rizwan

          Bait-e-Rizwan is also known as Bait-e-Shajra made under the Keekar tree.

          On this occasion, Muhammad gathered his nearly 1,400 Sahaba and called them to pledge to fight until death and revenge the death of Hazrat Usman

          How many times the Muslim have been commanded in the Quran for the performance of prayer(namaz)?

          while

          Salat was made compulsory in the 10th Hijri.

          Salat Juma became Farz in Madina 2H.

          227

          Who are the Sahibain?

          Correct Answer: Abu Yusuf and Imam Shaibani
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Two pupils of Imam Abu Hanifa, Imam Abu Yusuf and Imam Muhammad, are known as Sahibain.'

          They were Mujtahids in their own right due to their proficiency and expertise in :

          1. Fiqh
          2. Usul Fiqh
          3. Tafseer
          4. Usul Tafseer
          5. Hadith
          6. Ilm Hadith
          7. Naaskih and Mansukh
          8. Seerah
          9. Lugha 
          10. Balagha


          Additional Information

          Yaqub ibn Ibrahim al-Ansari known as Abu Yusuf

          Abu Yusuf's personal name was Yaqub.

          He was a student of jurist Abu Hanifah.



          ND-21-5-2023

          228

          Al-Muwatta is written by?

          Correct Answer: Imam Malik
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Muwaṭṭa of Imam Malik written in the 8th-century

          Al-Muwatta compiled In the Arabic language

          First published in 1989.

          ****

          Al-Muwatta contains approximately 1,720 hadith divided amongst the following hadith terminology as follows:

          1)      600 marfu hadith

          2)      613 mawquf  hadith

          3)      285 maqtu hadith

          4)      222 mursal  hadiths
