          ND-21-5-2023
          229

          Who was the first son of Hazrat Muhammad(PBHU)?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Qasim
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Qasim was the first (elder) son of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ and Hazrat Khadijah

          He died at the age of only 3 and was buried in Jannat al Mualla in Makkah.


          Additional Information:

          Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

          1. Hazrat Qasim 
          2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
          3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
          4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
          5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
          6. Hazrat Abdullah
          7. Hazrat Ibrahim 

          All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

          230

          Aad was the Nation of?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Hood
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Ād was an ancient tribe mentioned frequently in the Qurʾān.

          قبیلے کے ارکان، جنہیں عادات کہا جاتا ہے، ایک خوشحال قوم کی تشکیل کی یہاں تک کہ وہ ایک پرتشدد طوفان میں تباہ ہو گئے۔

          According to Islamic tradition, the storm came after they had rejected the teachings of a monotheistic prophet named Hud

          Ād is regarded as one of the original Arab tribes, the "lost Arabs".

          231

          Hazrat Abu Bakar was the merchant of?

          Correct Answer: Cloth
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Abu Bakr was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.

          He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

          His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.

          Hazrat Usman gave the idea to Hazrat Abu Bakar to collect the Quran in the form of a book

          At the suggestion of Hazrat Umar (RA), Hazrat Abu Bakr ( R.A) entrusted the task of compilation of the holy Quran to Zaid bin Sabit (RA).

          Holy Quran Collect in the reign of Hazrat Abubakar RA was done by Hazrat Zaid RA between 11 to 14 Hijri (732-34)

          ND-21-5-2023

          232

          Collection of Holy Quran in the reign of Hazrat Abubakar (RA) was done by Hazrat Zaid RA between ______?

          Correct Answer: 11-14 A.H
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Abu Bakr was a sahabi, one of the companions of Muhammad and the first Caliph of Islam.

          He was also Muhammad's father-in-law through Aisha.

          His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba and Abu Bakr was his kunya.

          Hazrat Usman gave the idea to Hazrat Abu Bakar to collect the Quran in the form of a book

          At the suggestion of Hazrat Umar (RA), Hazrat Abu Bakr ( R.A) entrusted the task of compilation of the holy Quran to Zaid bin Sabit (RA).

          Holy Quran Collect in the reign of Hazrat Abubakar RA was done by Hazrat Zaid RA between 11 to 14 Hijri (732-34)

          ND-21-5-2023

          233

          In the beginning of Madina's life, the Prophet Mohammed said the settlement of?

          Correct Answer: Banu Najjar
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          At the beginning of Madina's life, the Prophet Mohammed said the settlement of Banu Najjar

          The Prophet (peace be upon him) stayed at Abu Ayub's house for about seven months

          until the Prophet's Mosque was built on the open space where his camel had stopped.

          The camel stopped at the house of Abu Ayyub al-Ansari, a member of the Banu Najjar,, who was regarded as the best tribe of Medina.

          Abu Ayyub was born in Medina, Hejaz as Khalid bin Zayd to a Najjar family of the Banu Khazraj.

          ND-21-5-2023

          234

          The conquest of Mecca was on ____ 8 AH?

          Correct Answer: 20 Ramzan
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The conquest of Mecca by Muslims led by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in December 629 or January 630 AD (Julian), 20 Ramadan, 8 AH.

          The Messenger of Allah (S.A.W.) entered Makkah on his favorite camel Al-Kaswa

          having Usama Ibn Zaid (R.A.) sitting behind him

          At the time of the conquest of Makkah the Prophet Muhammad was accompanied by 10,000 companion 

          On his way, he recited Surah Al-Fatha (48th Surah of the Quran)

          The flag color of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) was white and yellow at the time of the conquest of Makkah

          ND18-4-2023

          235

          At the time of conquest of Makkah the Prophet Muhammad was accompanied _______ companion

          Correct Answer: 10,000
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The conquest of Mecca by Muslims led by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in December 629 or January 630 AD (Julian), 20 Ramadan, 8 AH.

          The Messenger of Allah (S.A.W.) entered Makkah on his favorite camel Al-Kaswa

          having Usama Ibn Zaid (R.A.) sitting behind him

          At the time of the conquest of Makkah the Prophet Muhammad was accompanied by 10,000 companion 

          On his way, he recited Surah Al-Fatha (48th Surah of the Quran)

          The flag color of the Holy Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) was white and yellow at the time of the conquest of Makkah

          ND18-4-2023

          236

          Laddy Haleema was the foster mother of Prophet who belonged to?

          Correct Answer: Banu Saad
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Halima Sadia was an Arabic Beduin woman.

          Foster mother of prophet Muhammad (PBUH)

          Halimah was from the tribe of Sa'd, a subdivision of Hawazin.

          Hazrat Halimah and her husband were from the tribe of Sa'd.

          For 6 years Haleema took care of Prophet (PBUH)

          Died: 630 CE in Madina

          ****

          3 foster mothers of the Holy Prophet

          1.        Hazrat Halema Saadia (RA)    
          2.      Hazrat Khola  (RA)
          3.        Hazrat Suwaiba (RA)
          ND-21-5-2023
          237

          Which night is better than thousand nights?

          Correct Answer: Lailat-ul-Qadar
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          Laylatul Qadr, the Night of Decree or Night of Power.

          Laylatul Qadr is better than 1 thousand months

          It takes place in the last ten days of Ramadan

          and was the night in which the Qur'an was revealed to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ

          observed as 21st, 23rd, 25th, 27th or 29th Ramada
          ND-21-5-2023

          238

          Who is the responsible for protection of the Holy Quran?

          Correct Answer: Allah
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          According to the Holy Quran: Allah has revealed the book and will protect it. 

          Allah, the Almighty, says in the Quran: “Indeed, it is We who sent down the Quran, and indeed, We will be its guardian.” (Surah Al-Hijr, 15:9).

          This verse is a testament to the divine promise that the Quran will remain unchanged and preserved in its original form.

          239

          Zakat cannot be given to?

          Correct Answer: All of these
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Zakat is backbon in Islamic economy.

          It is made compulsory in 2nd hijri

          8 masarf e zakat is mentioned in surah Tooba

          240

          Allah promised the Muslims?

          Correct Answer: Will cast terror into the hearts of infidels
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Surah e Anfal of the Holy Quran says:

          ‘And recall when your Lord inspired the angels: 'I am certainly with you. So make firm the feet of those who believe. I will cast terror into the hearts of those who disbelieve’

           

          241

          How many fasts did Esa (A.S) keep in the desert and advised His followers to follow them?

          Correct Answer: 40
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • 40 fasts did Esa (A.S) keep in the desert and advised His followers to follow it.
          • ***
          242

          Koofa was built on the orders of?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Koofa was built on the orders of Hazrat Ali (RA) in 36th Hijri.

          243

          Tripoli was conquered during the reign of?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Belonged

          Quraysh

          Born

          Makkah

          Caliph

          Third caliph

          Caliphate

          23 to 35 Hijjah

          Caliphate

          644 to 656 CE

          244

          Second belief of Islam

          Correct Answer: Angles
          Show explanation
          Explanation


          **********

          VU CBT FIA PAPER 25-12-2021

          245

          Who among the following established the Office of Qazi?

          Correct Answer: Umer (A.S)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Umer (A.S) established the office of qazi
          • ***
          • Umer (A.S) also established the same system as Army or police
          • **
          • The impartial justice system is one of the glaring examples of the caliphate of Hazrat Umar (RA)
          • he introduced an impartial justice system wherein every person was equal before the law,
          • There was no concept of rich or poor, noble or not-noble, white or black, Muslim or non-Muslim.
          246

          What is name of 16th Para of Holy Qur’an?

          Correct Answer: Qal Alam
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Total Para of Quran = 30
          Total Surah in Quran = 114
          Total Sajda in Quran = 14

          247

          Hazrat Usman (R.A) was born in

          Correct Answer: Makkah
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Belonged

          Quraysh

          Born

          Makkah

          Caliph

          Third caliph

          Caliphate

          23 to 35 Hijjah

          Caliphate

          644 to 656 CE

          Titles

          Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

          Martyred

          18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

          Martyrdom day

          Friday

          Wives

          Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

          Full Name

          Usman bin Affaan

          Death cause

          Martyred by rebels

          Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


          248

          Hazrat …..was martyred by Khalid Bin Waleed?

          Correct Answer: Hassan Bin Sabit (R.A)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Hazrat Hassan Bin Sabit (R.A) was martyred by Khalid Bin Waleed
          • ****
          • Khalid Bin Waleed (R.A) died in 627 AD.
          • Khalid Bin Waleed (R.A) fought more than 50 Battles in his life.
          • **
          • Hassan Bin Sabit (R.A) 35 Hijjah
          • Hazrat Ali (R .A) martyred at the age of 60

          249

          Who was appointed as Usher for Hijrat E Madina?

          Correct Answer: Abdullah Bin Ariqat (R.A)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          •  Abdullah Bin Ariqat (R.A) was appointed as Usher for Hijrat E Madina
          • ****
          • Usher means (رہنمائی کرنے والا) guider, representative.
          • ***

          related Question to Hijrat E Madina:

          • After Hijrat from Mecca to Madina, the Holy Prophet Muhammad’s (PBUH) camel kneeled at the door of: Hazrat Ayub Ansari
          • At the Hijrat e Madina which Surah is recited by Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) Surah e Yaseen
          • ***
          •  Prophet Muhammad’s migration from Mecca to Yathrib (Medina)on September 24, 622.
          • With Abu Bakar 
          • Hijra  from Makkah to Madina started in 13th Nabvi year.
          • Hijri year starts from this event.

          250

          who was the second son of Ibrahim (A.S)

          Correct Answer: Ishaq (A.S)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Hazrat Ishaq (A.S) was the second son of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S).
          • ***
          •  You can verify from Surah Shafaat, Ayat NO 101
          • Ibraham had two children, Ishmael and Isaac, who both later became prophets.
          • Ismail (A.S) from Hajra (A.S) and Ishaq (A.S) from Sara (A.S).
          • **

           A related question to Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S):

          •  Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq
          •  13 times Hazrat Ibrahim (علیہ) is mentioned in Sura Baqarah
          • Hazrat Ibrahim (AH) name mentioned in the Holy Quran 69 times.
          • Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)
          • Hazrat Hajirah the second wife of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)

          251

          Who is called Alexander of Muslim World?

          Correct Answer: Uqba bin Nafah (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Uqba ibn Nafi was an Arab general serving the Rashidun Caliphate since the Reign of Umar and later on the Umayyad Caliphate during the reigns of Muawiyah I and Yazid I, leading the Muslim conquest of the Maghreb, including present-day Algeria, Tunisia, Libya and Morocco.
          252

          The disciplined universal is the best proof of ------.

          Correct Answer: Tauheed
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The disciplined universal is the best proof of Tauheed.
          • **
          • Quran says about Tawhid
          • Tawhid: At the center of all Islamic belief is the belief in one God—Allah.
          • There is no other God but Allah and there is no one comparable to Him.
          • We will never understand Allah as His nature supersedes our limited minds.
          • We are made by Him but He is not Himself made.
          253

          Universal Resalat is the quality of -----?

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Universal Resalat is the quality of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH).
          • **
          • رسالت,( resalat ) means prophethood, apostleship.
          • This is the adjective form of the noun 'Rasool' which means prophet.
          • **
          • Risalah is the Arabic word for “message”.
          • The responsibility of “Rasool” is Risalat.
          • “Risalat” can also be thought of as delivering the message of ALLAH(SAW) to the people.
          254

          The first revelation of the Holy Quran consisted of ----Aayats.

          Correct Answer: five
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The first revelation of the Holy Quran consisted of 5 Aayats.
          • **
          • Related questions about the revelation of the Holy Quran:
          • 2 years and six months was the interval between the first and the second revelation of the Holy Quran
          • 1st revelation of Muhammad was made in Hira. On 17 Ramzan 610 AD 
          • 23 years did it take for the complete revelation of the Quran
          • Prophet (SAW) used to meditate on Mount Hira before revelation.
          • According to Islamic Calendar, on the 21st of Ramadan the prophet Muhammad(PBUH) received the first revelations of the Quran
          • **
          • The Quran was verbally revealed from God to Muhammad through the angel Gabriel beginning on 22 December 609 CE,
          • when Muhammad was 40 and concluding in 632 CE, the year of his death.
          255

          the bases of our national unity are -------.

          Correct Answer: Islam
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • the bases of our national unity are Islam.
          • **
          • national unity in Islam
          • Islamic law rests on the precipice of Al-Waḥdah (Unity) which includes.
          • unity of humankind
          • unity between human beings and his. environment
          • unity within the family
          • unity of the state and society, of. government and politics, economy and culture, and law and policy.
          256

          If fast is deliberately broken, how many fasts are are kept to make up for it?

          Correct Answer: 60
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Fasting was made obligatory in second Hijri. 

          257

          Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) was buried a the time of:

          Correct Answer: Evening
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) was buried in Madina (571 AD) 

          258

          Who led the burial prayer of Hazrat Ali (RA)?

          Correct Answer: Hassan (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Ali (R.A) was attacked with a poisoned sword On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri,

          before the time of FAJAR prayers to the mosque of Kufa.

          He died on the 21st of Ramadan 40 A.H.

          He was buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).

          259

          Minimum threshold of wealth for Zakat is called:

          Correct Answer: Nisab
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Nisab of zakat for silver is 52 1/2 tola

          Zakat literally means Purification.

          When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H.

          260

          The birth name of Maymuna bint Haris (RA) is___________?

          Correct Answer: Barra
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The birth name of Hazrat Maymunah is Barra 

          Muhammad changed it to Maymunah, meaning "good tidings"

          She was the daughter of Hazrat Harith (RA)

          Hazrat Memona was the tenth wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

          She was 36 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was 60 years ago

          She died in 51 AH




          261

          Which one is called Mashar -ul- Haram?

          Correct Answer: Muzdalifah valley
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The border of Muzdalifah is marked by a large purple signpost.

          There is  also a masjid called Mashar Al Haram, which means "holy monument". 

          262

          What was the old Name of Saudi Arabia?

          Correct Answer: Najd And Hejaz
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Know everything about Saudi Arabia
          • Location: Southeast Asia
          • Capital: Riyadh
          • Currency: Riyal
          • Neom City Project: Saudi Arabia
          • Parliament: Majlis Al-Shura
          • Longest river: Wadi al-Rummah
          • largest country of: Arabian Peninsula
          • Old names: Najd and Hejaz

           

           

           

          263

          Which surah is on the name of metal

          Correct Answer: Surah Al Hadid
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Al-Ḥadīd  is the 57th chapter  of the Quran.

           29 verses.

          The chapter takes its name from that word which appears in the 25th verse.


          264

          Which is the signature event of the 6th Hijri?

          Correct Answer: Treaty of Sulah Hudaibiya
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Treaty of Sulah Hudaibiya is the signature event of the 6th Hijri
          • **
          • Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (رضہ)
          • Sulah e Hudaibiya Was Signed In The Year 628/ 6H.
          • Sule e Huabaibiya Was Signed For 10 Years.
          • Hudabia is the name of the tree.
          • Sulah e Hudaibiya is called Fatah Al-Mubeen in Surah Al-Fatah
          • Hazrat Ali (R.A) wrote the Treaty of Hudaibiya
          • ***
          • Gazwa e badr was fought in 2 hijri,13 March 622 AD
          265

          Name the Mosque where almost 70 prophets are buried

          Correct Answer: Masjid Al Hanif
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Masjid e Hanif is in Mina ,

          a neighbourhood of Mecca

           Distance from Mecca 5 km

          266

          Hazart Abu Zar Ghafari (RA) belongs to Banu Ghifar The kinanah Tribe and he died in ________A.D.

          Correct Answer: 652 (Ad)
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          He belonged to the Banu Ghifar, the Kinanah tribe. He died in 652 CE, at al-Rabadha, in the desert east of Medina. Abu Dhar is remembered for his strict piety and also his opposition to Muawiyah I during the caliph Uthman ibn Affan era.
          267

          For which was Maiz Aslami punished in Prophet's (PBUH) time?

          Correct Answer: Adultry
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          •  Adultery was Maiz Aslami was punished in Prophet's (PBUH) time
          • **
          • Abu Huraira (ra) said:
            • Once Maiz Aslami (ra) appeared before the Prophet (PBUH) and bore witness against himself 4 times that he had committed adultery with a woman every time he made his statement the Prophet turned his face away from Maiz.
            • After the fourth time, the Prophet took action and he was ordered to be stoned to death. 
          268

          The marriage ceremony of The Holy Prophet (PBUH) and Maymuna a bint Haris (RA) held_______?

          Correct Answer: 7 A.H
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The marriage ceremony of Hazrat Memona and Hazrat Muhammad held on 7 AH

          Hazrat Memona was the tenth wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

          She was 36 years old at the time of Nikah and Muhammad ﷺ was 60 years ago

          The birth name of Hazrat Memona is Barra

          She lived with Muhammadﷺ for three years until his death

          She died in 51 AH


          Additional Information

          She was the daughter of Hazrat Harith (RA)

          She was first married to Abu Ruhm ibn Abd al-Uzza. 


          269

          Which surah is called surah-e-Shukr?

          Correct Answer: Surah Fatiha
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Surah fatiha is called surah-e-Shukr

          ..

          1 Rukus in surah Al-FAtiha

          surah Al Fatiah first surah of Holy Quran

           7 Verses are in Surah “Al Fatiha”

          surah Al Fatiha No. of words: 29

          surah Al Fatiha No. of letters: 139

          surah Al Fatiah is also called Umm Al-Kitab ("the Mother of the Book") and Umm Al-Quran ("the Mother of the Quran");


          ND16-8-2023

          270

          Who became first Muslim woman deputy mayor of British town?

          Correct Answer: Tafheen Sharif
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          In May 2022, Tafheen Sharif of Pakistani origin elected the first Muslim woman deputy mayor of Tameside in the UK.

          Tafheen Sharif belongs: Labour party

          271

          On silver Falqaram (Nisab) is ___?

          Correct Answer: 52 1/2 Tolas
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Nisab on silver is 52 1/2 Tolas
          • ..
          • If you have 7.5 tola/3 ounces/87.48 grams of gold or
          • 52.5 tota/21 ounces/612.36 grams of silver or
          • its cash equivalent for a full lunar year,
          • then you are considered Sahib-e-Nisab and should pay Zakat.
          272

          ______________ died first among the Sahabah.

          Correct Answer: Hazrat Asad bin Zarra (RA)
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Hazrat Asad ( RA) died first among the Sahabah.
          • ..
          • Hazrat Asad ( RA) died within days, at the beginning of Shawwal, i.e., in April 623.
          • Hazrat Asad ( RA) was the son of Hashim ibn Abd al-Manaf and the brother of Abdul Muttalib.
          • Hazrat Asad ( RA) was a companion of the Islamic prophet Muhammad and the first chief in Medina


          273

          Hazrat Usman (RA) was martyred on:

          Correct Answer: Friday
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Belonged

          Quraysh

          Born

          Makkah

          Caliph

          Third caliph

          Caliphate

          23 to 35 Hijjah

          Caliphate

          644 to 656 CE

          Titles

          Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

          Martyred

          18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

          Martyrdom day

          Friday

          Wives

          Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

          Full Name

          Usman bin Affaan

          Death cause

          Martyred by rebels

          Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


          274

          Hazrat Usman (RA) remained third caliph of Islam from _______ Hijjah to ______ Hijjah.

          Friday

          Wives

          Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

          Usman bin Affaan

          Death cause

          Martyred by rebels

          Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

          Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

          Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

          After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

          Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

          Hazrat Usman (RA) used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

          Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

           Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

          Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


          278

          The dead body of Muslim male is wrapped in _______sheets.

          Correct Answer: Three
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          "The dead body of Muslim male is wrapped in three sheets,

          The dead body of Muslim women is wrapped in five sheets."

          279

          Name the Nafli salat which can be offered after sunrise till on forth of the day?

          Correct Answer: Chasht
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Salat al-Dhuha (Chasht) can be offered after sunrise, approximately 15 minutes after, and ends before midday when the sun reaches its zenith.

          280

          Tragedy of Karbala took place in ________ A.D.

          Correct Answer: 680
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          The Battle of Karbala was fought on 10 October 680 between the army of the second Umayyad caliph Yazid I and a small army led by Husayn ibn Ali, , at Karbala, Iraq.
          281

          The duty of Hazrat Mickaeel is:

          Correct Answer: Responsible of Rain and wind
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Hazrat Mickaeel is  Responsible of Rain and wind.

          Hazrat Jibrael (علیہ) delivered the Message to every Prophet from Hazrat Adam to Muhammad (pbuh).

          Izra'il – The Angel of Death, who takes the souls from bodies when people die.

          Israfil – The angel who will be present on the day of resurrection .

          Mīkāl_ is often depicted as providing nourishment for bodies and souls while also being responsible for bringing rain and thunder to Earth.

          282

          What is the literal meaning of Nabi?

          Correct Answer: A person who conveys the message of Allah
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          There are 124000 nabi in islam                                                                

          4 Rasool have holy books:

          1) Hazrat Musa(A.S)  (Book:   Toraat)                                                 

          2) Hazrat Daood(A.S) (Book : Zaboor)                                               

          3) Hazrat Issa(A.S) (Book:  Anjeel)                                                   

          4) Hazrat Muhammad (S.A.W) (Book: Quran e majeed)

          283

          Hazrat Talha r.a got how many injuries in Ghazwa e uhad?

          Correct Answer: 70
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Ghazwa uhad fought on 3rd hijri,

          women participated first time in ghwza uhad,

          Teeth Of Prophet Hazrat Muhammad PBUH was shahed in battle of Uhad.

          Abu Sufyan commander the infidels in  Uhad Ghazwa

          ***

          It was fought in 3 Hijri 

          Ohad is the name of the mountain

          3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad

          Abdullah ibn Ubayy decided to withdraw his 300 men before the battle of Uhad. 

          The Battle of Uhud was fought on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD, in the valley north of Mount Uhud.

          1000 Muslims participated in the battle of Uhad

          3000 Infidels participated in the battle of Uhad Infidel means کفار
          In the battle of Uhad Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) selected 50Skillful archers to stay on the mountain

          *****

            ND13-1-2023

            284

            Al hadi is the name of Allah, what is its meaning?

            Correct Answer: The Guide
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Allah is Al Haadi, the One who gives guidance to his believers.

            ....

            Allah calls Himself An-Naseer— The Helper

            ND16-8-2023

            285

            The Holy Prophet (PBUH) used to fast on:

            Correct Answer: Monday and Thursday
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Holy Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) used to break his fast with dates and if there were no dates he would break his fast with a few sips of water.

            286

            Name the term on which acting upon it is not rewarding but it is also not a sin if you do not act upon it what is about it?

            Correct Answer: Mubah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mubah is an Arabic word meaning "permitted", which has technical uses in Islamic law.

            Question in Urdu

            اس اصطلاح کا نام دیں جس پر عمل کرنا فائدہ مند نہیں ہے لیکن یہ بھی گناہ نہیں ہے اگر آپ اس پر عمل نہیں کرتے ہیں تو اس کے بارے میں کیا ہے؟

            Mubah/ اجازت نامہ
            287

            What Caliphate came before the Abbasid Caliphate?

            Correct Answer: Umayyad Caliphate
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Abbasid caliphate, second of the two great dynasties of the Muslim empire of the caliphate.

            It overthrew the Umayyad caliphate in 750 ce and reigned as the Abbasid caliphate until it was destroyed by the Mongol invasion in 1258.


            *********

            Remember Tips

            یاد کرنے کے لیے

            1. Rashidin -30 years
            2. Umayyad -100 years
            3. Abbasid - 500 years
            4. Ottoman Empire - 600 years


            288

            Hejaz and Najd are the old names of the country:

            Correct Answer: Saudi Arabia
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The area of modern-day Saudi Arabia formerly consisted of mainly four distinct regions: Hejaz, Najd and parts of Eastern Arabia (Al-Ahsa) and Southern Arabia (‘Asir).


            289

            The ninth year of the Hijra is known in Muslim history as:

            Correct Answer: Year of deputations
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The ninth year of the Hijra is known as the "Year of Deputations" when delegates came from all over Arabia to enter into Islam.

            290

            Surah Bani Israel is also known as:

            Correct Answer: Al-Isra
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Surah Bani Isreal is also known as Surah Al-Isra.

            Surah Bani Isreal has 111 Ayats.

            291

            Al-Isra is the second name of which Surah in the Holy Quran?

            Correct Answer: Surah Bani Israil
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Surah Bani Isreal is also known as Surah Al-Isra.

            Surah Bani Isreal has 111 Ayats.

            292

            Which surah contains information about Wazu, Tayyamum ,etc?

            Correct Answer: Al Maida
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al-Ma'idah is the fifth chapter of the Quran, with 120 verses.

            293

            Which Ummul Momineen was the daughter of Abu Sufyan?

            Correct Answer: Umm e Habiba
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Umm-ul-Momineen Bibi Umm-e-Habiba

            Wife of

            The Holy Prophet (PBUH)

            Real Name

            Ramla

            Daughter of

            Abu Sufyan

            Umm-ul-Momineen

            Mother of believers

            294

            Which surah is known as Asas Al Quran?

            Correct Answer: Fatiha
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Surah Fatiha, the first Surah of the Holy Quran is also known as Asass-al-Quran.

            295

            Hazrat Bibi Fatima (RA) was the daughter of Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) and ________?

            Correct Answer: Khadija bint Khuwaylid
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Bibi Fatima is 4th daughter and 5th child of Hazrat Muhammad and Hazrat Khadija (RA)

            She was born in Makkah on the 20th of Jumada al-Thaniya 18 B.H.

            She married Hazrat Ali RA ( 4th Rashidun Caliphs) in 2 AH

            She had 2 sons and 2 daughters: 

            1. Imam Hussain
            2. Imam Hassan
            3. Zaynab 
            4. Umm Kulthum

            Title of Bibi Fatima is: Tahira, Zahra, Umm Abiha, Sayyidatu nisa-alamin and al-Batul

            She died in Madina on 11/632 

            She was buried in Jannat-ul-Baqi


            Additional Information:

            Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
            1. Hazrat Qasim 
            2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
            3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
            4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
            5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
            6. Hazrat Abdullah
            7. Hazrat Ibrahim 



            296

            Hazrat Fatima (R.A) is known as_________?

            Correct Answer: both B. and C)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Bibi Fatima (RA)

            Born

            Makkah

            Father

            The Holy prophet (PBHU)

            Mother

            Hazrat Khadija

            Husband

            Hazrat Ali (RA)

            Sons

            Imam Hussain & Imam Hassan

            Titles

            Tahira, Zahra, Umm Abiha, Sayyidatu nisa-`alamin and al-Batul

            Died

            Madina

            Born

            Makkah

            300

            Hazrat Bibi Fatima (RA) was the daughter of_______?

            Correct Answer: The Holy Prophet PBUH
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Bibi Fatima is 4th daughter and 5th child of Hazrat Muhammad and Hazrat Khadija (RA)

            She was born in Makkah on the 20th of Jumada al-Thaniya 18 B.H.

            She married Hazrat Ali RA ( 4th Rashidun Caliphs) in 2 AH

            She had 2 sons and 2 daughters: 

            1. Imam Hussain
            2. Imam Hassan
            3. Zaynab 
            4. Umm Kulthum

            Title of Bibi Fatima is: Tahira, Zahra, Umm Abiha, Sayyidatu nisa-alamin and al-Batul

            She died in Madina on 11/632 

            She was buried in Jannat-ul-Baqi


            Additional Information:

            Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.
            1. Hazrat Qasim 
            2. Hazrat Zainab (RA)
            3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) 
            4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) 
            5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) 
            6. Hazrat Abdullah
            7. Hazrat Ibrahim

            Tahira and Zahra are the titles of:

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Fatima
            Show explanation

            Born

            Makkah

            303

            Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased a land for 25,000 dirham to extend:

            Correct Answer: Masjid-e-Nabavi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Usman (R.A)

            Belonged

            Quraysh

            Born

            Makkah

            Caliph

            Third caliph

            Caliphate

            23 to 35 Hijjah

            Caliphate

            644 to 656 CE

            Titles

            Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

            Martyred

            18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

            Martyrdom day

            Friday

            Wives

            Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

            Full Name

            Usman bin Affaan

            Death cause

            Martyred by rebels

            Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

            Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

            Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

            Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

            After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

            Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

            Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

            Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

            Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

             Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

            Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


            304

            Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in __________ because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

            Correct Answer: Battle of Badr
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Usman (R.A)

            Belonged

            Quraysh

            Born

            Makkah

            Caliph

            Third caliph

            Caliphate

            23 to 35 Hijjah

            Caliphate

            644 to 656 CE

            Titles

            Zun-Noorain & Jame-al-Quran

            Martyred

            18th Zul-Hijjah 35 A.H

            Martyrdom day

            Friday

            Wives

            Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA)

            Full Name

            Usman bin Affaan

            Death cause

            Martyred by rebels

            Know everything about Hazrat Usman (R.A)

            Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

            Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

            Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

            After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

            Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

            Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

            Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

            Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

             Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

            Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran. 


            305

            Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of:

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Ruqayyah was the Second daughter and third child of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ 

            Hazrat Umm-e-Kalthum was 3rd daughter and 4th child of the Holy Prophet ﷺ 

            After the death of her sister Ruqayya left Uthman a widower, he married Umm Kulthum

            Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in the Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on his deathbed.

            Ruqayyah (RA died on the victory day of the Battle of Badr.




            306

            Who persuaded Hazrat Usman (RA) to accept Islam?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat AbuBakar (RA)

            Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

            Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

            Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

            After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

            Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

            Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

            Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

            Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

             Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

            Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran.

            310

            Who conquered island Cyprus

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Uthman, also spelled Osman, was the third Rashidun caliph, ruling from November 644 until his assassination.

            311

            Who adopted the title of Ameer-ul-Momineen for the first time?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Umar (RA)  adopted the title of Ameer-ul-Momineen for the first time.

            On his accession in 634, Umar ibn Khattab, the Second Rashidun Caliph, was given the title.

            According to At-Tabaqat al Kubra, When Abu Bakr died, Muslims of the time said: “We are the Mu'minin and Umar is our Amir (Commander).”

            After this, the title Amir al-Mu'minin was held by Umar ibn Khattab who was also the first one to be given this title.

            Sh19-8-2023


            312

            Hazrat Khadija and Hazrat Abu Talib died in?

            Correct Answer: 619 A.D
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Khadijah died in Ramadan of the year 10 after the Prophethood, i.e., in November AD 619, Prophetﷺ uncle and protector Abu Talib died same year.

            Muhammad later called this tenth year the Year of Sorrow,

            313

            Sahib Us-Ser is the name of:

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Khuzaifa (R.A)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Huzaifah was one of the Sahabah (companion) of the Islamic prophet, Muhammad

            Died:c. 656 AD Al-Mada'in, Iraq

            Military service
            AllegianceMuhammad (623–632)
            Umar (634–644)
            Battles/wars
            • Under Muhammad:
              • Battle of Uhud (625)
              • Battle of the Trench (626)
            • Under Umar:
              • The Muslim Conquest of Persia
                • Battle of Burs (636)
                • Battle of Babylon (636)
                • Siege of Ctesiphon (637)
                • Battle of Nahavand (642)
            ND18-5-2023

            314

            Which one is called Mansha’sul Haram?

            Correct Answer: Makkah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mashar al-Haram means the 'Sacred Monument'

            Masjid Mashar al-Haram (Arabic: مسجد مشعر الحرام) is a mosque situated in Muzdalifah 

            Muzdalifah is the area where the Prophet ﷺ supplicated during his Farewell Pilgrimage.

            Muzdalifah is located midway between Masjid al-Khayf in Mina and Masjid al-Namirah in Arafat.


            ND18-5-2023
            315

            The holy prophet Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) died on the day of:

            Correct Answer: Monday
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH) Born on c. 570 CE (53 BH) Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia

            He Died  on June 8, 632 on Monday at the age of 63 in the city of Madina.

            316

            Minimum Persons required for Namaz Ba-Jamaat?

            Correct Answer: 2
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Two Person is very necessary for Ba-Jamaat.
            317

            The name of lbn-e- Hisham's famous book is:

            Correct Answer: Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah

            • As-Sīrah an-Nabawiyyah (السيرة النبوية), 'The Life of the Prophet'; is an edited recension of Ibn Isḥāq's classic Sīratu Rasūli l-Lāh (سيرة رسول الله) 'The Life of God's Messenger'.
            • Ibn Isḥāq's now lost work survives only in Ibn Hishām's and al-Tabari's recensions, although fragments of several others survive, and Ibn Hishām and al-Tabarī share virtually the same material.
            318

            How many Surahs are in 30th Chapter (Parah) of Quran?

            Correct Answer: 37
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Total Surah in Quran = 114
            Total Parah in Quran = 30
            Total Ayat =             6666

            Rakoh= 558
            319

            What is the Ratio of Zakat in grams on Gold?

            Correct Answer: 87.48 grams of gold
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • If you have 7.5 tola/3 ounces/87.48 grams of gold or
            • 52.5 tota/21 ounces/612.36 grams of silver or
            • its cash equivalent for a full lunar year,
            • then you are considered Sahib-e-Nisab and should pay Zakat 2.5%
            320

            Which of the following has the holiest places for Muslims?

            Correct Answer: Saudi Arabia
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Makkah and Madina, both holiest places for Muslims located in Saudi Arabia.

            Hajj is also performed in Saudi Arabia.

            321

            Hazrat ___had knowledge of dreams

            Correct Answer: Abu bakar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Abu Bakar (R.A) had the knowledge of dreams. and Prophets Hazrat Yusuf (R.A) was an expert in the knowledge of dreams

            • Abu Bakr (R.A) was the first Caliph of Islam.
            • He was also Muhammad's father-in-law and father of Hazrat Aisha.
            • His real name was Abdullah or Abul-Kaaba, and Abu Bakr was his kunya.

            Last updated by NS on 06 May 2026

            322

            Who was the first host of the Holy Prophet in Madina ____?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Ayoub Ansari
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            Abu Ayyub al-Ansari أبو أيوب الأنصاري

            The patronym Abu Ayyub, means father (abu) of Ayyub. Abu Ayyub died of illness during the First Arab Siege of Constantinople.

            Born Yathrib, Hejaz, Arabia

            Died c. 674 Constantinople, Byzantine Empire

            Relations Banu Khazraj (Banu Najjar


            323

            Who was the first key-holder of Kaba among Quraish?

            Correct Answer: Qussai
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Qusay in Islam
            Qusayy ibn Kilab ibn Murra (Arabic: قصي ٱبن كلاب ٱبن مرة, romanized:
            Quṣayy ibn Kilāb ibn Murra; c. 400–480) was the 5th-century ruler of Mecca, who established the Quraysh as the dominant power in Mecca.
            Following his death, a succession crisis arose between his sons Abd al-Dar and al-Mughira.
            324

            Our Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW) had ___________ brothers and sisters.

            Correct Answer: None
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            No actual brother and sister .

            Hamzah ibn ʿAbd al-Muṭṭalib was a foster brother, companion and paternal uncle of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

            325

            Who wept too much over the destruction of his nation that he got blinded_________?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Shoaib (AS)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Musa is revered as a prominent prophet and messenger in Islam, and his narrative is recounted the most among the prophets in the Quran.

            He is regarded by Muslims as one of the five most prominent prophets in Islam, along with Jesus (Isa), Abraham (Ibrahim), Noah (Nuh) and Muhammad.


            6 Miracles of Prophet Musa A.S given by Allah.

            Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA) donated all his property in the battle of:

            What was the interval between the first and the second revelation of Holy Quran?

            Correct Answer: 2 years and six months

            The Quran was verbally revealed from God to Muhammad through the angel Gabriel beginning on 22 December 609 CE, when Muhammad was 40, and concluding in 632 CE, the year of his death.

            Correct Answer: 11 A.H

            Prophet was the commander in the expedition of Tabuk.

            Number of hadiths narrated by Hazrat Abu Huraira R.A?

            Correct Answer: 5374
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Abu Hurairah R.A wrote narrations of 5374 Ahadis

            Abu Hurairah continued collecting hadith after the death of:

            Muhammad from Abu Bakr,

            Umar,

            Aisha,

            Fadl ibn Abbas,

            Usama ibn Zayd,

            Ubayy ibn Ka'b,

            and Ka'b al-Ahbar.

            332

            Pick out the name of the Pious Caliph who took over the control of the state of Madina in 632 AD and died two years later?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abubakar (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Hazrat Abubakar (RA) was the first Pious Caliph of Islam.

             Hazrat Abubakar (RA) became caliph in 632 AD.

             Hazrat Abubakar (RA) died in 634 AD.

             Hazrat Abubakar (RA) was also the father in law of the Holy prophet (PBUH).

            ++++++

            The Pious Caliphs were the first four Caliphs.

            They were:  Abu Bakr  Umar  Uthman  Ali

            RK20.5.2023

            Name (in Arabic)Born (CE)Reigned from (CE)DiedRelationship with MuhammadHouse
            Abu Bakr (أبو بكر الصديق)5738 June 63222 August 634Father of Aisha,  Muhammad's wifeBanu Taim
            Umar (عمر بن الخطاب)58423 August 6343 November 644 (assassinated by Abu Lu'lu'a Firuz)Father of Hafsa,  Muhammad's wifeBanu Adi
            Uthman (عثمان بن عفان)57911 November 64420 June 656 (assassinated at the end of a siege upon his house; see First Fitna)Husband of Muhammad's daughters, Ruqayya and later Umm Kulthum, and grandson of Muhammad's paternal auntBanu Ummaya
            Ali (علي بن أبي طالب)60120 June 656 29 January 661    (assassinated while praying in the Mosque of Kufa; see First Fitna)Muhammad's cousin, and husband of Fatimah, Muhammad's daughter and Umamah bint Zainab, Muhammad's granddaughterBanu Hashim

            Name the mountain where the Holy Prophet (S.A.W) deputed 50 skilful archers (Teer-Andaaz) in the battle of Ohad?

            Correct Answer: Jabel-e-Yahnene
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Jabal al-Rumah, also known as Jabal Ainain, is a small mountain located near Jabal Uhud where Muslim archers were positioned during the Battle of Uhud.
            336

            What is called the seven times walks around the Kaaba?

            Correct Answer: Tawaf
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            The Tawaf is a religious ritual that involves walking around the Kaaba seven times counterclockwise.

            It is an obligatory part of the Hajj and Umrah pilgrimages.

             The northern area where pilgrims walk is called the head.

            337

            Name the Sahabi who is a cousin and son in law of the Holy Prophet (SAW)?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Fatimah was married in AD 624 to Ali ibn Abi Talib (l. AD 601-661), a cousin of Prophet Muhammad, and the marriage lasted to the end of her days.
            • Hazrat Ali (R.A) was attacked with a poisoned sword On 19th Ramazan 40th Hijri,
            • before the time of FAJAR prayers to the mosque of Kufa.
            • He died on the 21st of Ramadan 40 A.H.
            • He was buried in Najaf al-Ashraf (Iraq).
            338

            Who was the first Muhajir of Madina?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Salam (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hazrat Abu Salam (RA)
            • Abdullah ibn Salam was a Jew in Yathrib who belonged to the Banu Qaynuqa tribe and claimed descent from Joseph.
            • He was widely respected and honored by the people of the city, including those who were not Jewish. He was known for his piety and goodness, righteous conduct and truthfulness.
            339

            What portion of war spoils should be deposited in Bait ul Maal???

            Correct Answer: 115
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Bayt al-mal (بيت المال) is an Arabic term that is translated as "House of money" or "House of Wealth." Historically,

            it was a financial institution responsible for the administration of taxes in Islamic states, particularly in the early Islamic Caliphate

            340

            4th source of Islamic law?

            Correct Answer: Qiyas
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • fourth source of Sharia
            • The primary sources of Islamic law are the Holy Book (The Quran), The Sunnah (the traditions or known practices of the Prophet Muhammad ), Ijma' (Consensus), and Qiyas (Analogy)
            • Qiyas or analogical reason is the fourth source of Sharia for the majority of Sunni jurisprudence. It aims to draw analogies to a previously accepted decision
            341

            Khyber was a stronghold of ?

            Correct Answer: Jews
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            There is a fort known as 'Khyber' 64 km to the west of medina (Saudi Arabia). This was a stronghold of Jews in the pre-Islamic days

            342

            Who was the first one to embrace Islam in the land of Yemen?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Moosa Ashari (RA)
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Hazrat Abu Moosa al-Ashari (RA) was indeed a significant early convert to Islam from Yemen
            • While not the very first individual from Yemen to embrace Islam
            • Appointed as the governors over Yemen by the Prophet (peace be upon him)
            • Serve in that role until the caliphate of Abu Bakr.

            NOTE

            Hazrat Zoaib bin Kalaib (RA) played a role in the spread of Islam, but the first person to bring Islam to Yemen was not him.

             Hazrat Muadh ibn Jabal (RA), who was sent by the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to Yemen to teach Islam and collect Zakat.


            343

            According to Islamic Calendar,on which day of Ramadan did the prophet Muhammad(PBUH) received the first revelations of the Quran?

            Correct Answer: 21st
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            Muhammad's first revelation was an event taking place in 610 AD, during which the Islamic prophet,

            Muhammad was visited by the angel Jibril, who revealed to him the beginnings of what would later become the Qur’an.

            Saudi Arabia closed Masjid Al Kheyf due to COVID-19 in 2020 and reopened it on 15 June 2022.

            Al Khyef mosque is located in Mina, Makkah.

            In Pakistan. Ushr began to be collected from the

            Correct Answer: Rabi crop of 1982-83
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Ushr (literally that means one tenth) is levied at the produce of landowners exceeding 948 kilograms of wheat or different crops of the identical cost. year-clever assessment on real collection of ushr among 1982-83 and 1992-93 can be seen at Appendix II. Ushr quantity does now not form a part of the vital zakah fund.
            • The neighborhood zakat committees were accountable for this paintings as voluntary companies, at instances unaware of the significance of the task and lacking the know-how of sales matters.
            • local impacts additionally obstruct the challenge of evaluation and collection. Resultantly, ushr which can were a main source of providing help to the deserving individuals inside the rural areas has failed in accomplishing the goal.
            361

            In which surat of Quran there is mentioned of 'Zulqurnain':

            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al Kahaf

            Dhu alQarnayn appears in the Qur'an, Surah al-Qarph, and Ayas 83-101 as a person who travels  east and west and builds a barrier between  certain people and Gog and Magog. Elsewhere, the Qur'an tells how  the release of Gog and Magog from behind the barrier signals the end of the world.

            367

            How many verses in the Holy Quran dealing with animals?

            Correct Answer: 200
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            There are more than 200 verses in Quran dealing with Animals.

            Six Chapters of Quran are named after animals or insects.

            The biggest animal mentioned in the Holy Quran is Elephant

            Following Surah with the name of Animals.

            1. Surah al Baqarh (The cow)
            2. Surah al Anaam (The cattle)
            3. Sureh al Nahl (the Bees)
            4. Sureh al Namal (The Ant)
            5. Sureh Al Ankbut ( The Spider)
            6. Surah Al Fil ( The elephant)


            368

            Name the brother of Hazrat Yousuf (A.S) to whom Hazrat Yousuf a.s disclosed that he was his lost brother?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Binyamin (A.S)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Prophet Yusuf (AS) had 11 brothers.

            Prophet Yusuf (AS) was the son of Prophet Ya'qub (AS).

            Prophet Yusuf (AS) once dreamt that eleven stars and the sun and moon were prostrating to him.

            Hazrat Binyamin (A.S) the brother of Hazrat Yousuf (A.S) to whom Hazrat yousuf a.s disclosed that he was his lost brother.


            369

            Additional Takbeers are offered in which of the following prayer?

            Correct Answer: Salat-e-Eid
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            عید الفطر اور عید الأضحى کی دو رکعات نماز جو چھہ زائد تکبیرات سے ادا کی جاتی ہے. پہلی رکعت میں ثنا کے بعد قرأت سے پہلے تین (٣) زائد تکبیرات کہی جاتی ہیں، اور دوسری رکعت میں قرأت کے بعد تین (٣) زائد تکبیرات کہہ کر رکوع کی تکبیر کہتے رکوع میں چلے جاتے ہیں.

            370

            In Battle of Khaybar, Jew warrior Marhab was killed by?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Jew warrior Marhab came out to fight Imam ‘Ali, who had already killed two of his brothers before. The fight lasted for some time, and finally, Imam Ali (R.A) killed him. He then attacked the party accompanying Marhab, and during this fight, his shield fell to the ground.

             

            371

            Who martyred Abdullah Bin Zubair R.A?

            Correct Answer: Hajjaj bin Yousuf
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Ibn al-Zubayr was left in peace until 692, when the caliph ʿAbd al-Malik sent an army to Mecca to force him to submit.

            Place of death: Mecca
            Place of birth:             Medina
            372

            How many Muslims got martyrdom in the battle of Khyber?

            Correct Answer: 15
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            15 Muslims got martyrdom in the battle of Khyber

            93 Jews were killed in the Battle of Khyber

             Ghazwa e khyber was fought on 7 Hijri

            In Ghazwa e khyber 3 caliphs were martyred due to the lake of water

            The Battle of Khaybar was fought in the year 628 between Muslims and the Jews living in the oasis of Khaybar,

            The Battle of Khaybar Start date: 629 AD
            The Battle of Khaybar Location:             Khaybar, Saudi Arabia
            The Battle of Khaybar End date:             629 AD
            The Battle of Khaybar Result:             Muslim victory

            ND-5-09--023



            373

            The number of surah e Alaq in the Holly Quran according to present arrangement.

            Correct Answer: 96
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al-ʻAlaq or The Clot  also known as "The Clinging Thing" or "The Embryo"

             is the 96th chapter (sūrah) of the Qur'an.

            It is composed of 19 āyāt or verses.

            It is sometimes also known as Sūrat Iqrā

            1st 5 verses of surah Alaq revealed as 1st wahi  in Gar e Hira

            Classification: Meccan

            No. of Sajdahs: verse 19

            ND18-5-2023
            374

            What was the original color of Hajr-e-Aswad?

            Correct Answer: White
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            What is Istelam?

            Kissing Hajre Aswad

             What was the age of the Holy Prophet at the time of Hajr-e-Aswad incident?

            35 years

            ****

            سنن ترمذي حدیث نمبر ( 877 ) سنن نسائ حدیث نمبر ( 2935 ) امام ترمذی رحمہ اللہ تعالٰی نے اسے حدیث کوصحیح قرار دیا ہے ۔ 2 - حجراسود دودھ سے بھی زیادہ سفید تھا جسے اولاد آدم کے گناہوں نے سیاہ کر دیا ہے :      

            375

            Abu jahal was killed in the battle of_____?

            Correct Answer: Badar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Abu Jahal was killed in the battle of Badar

            Abu Jahl was fatally wounded by Muadh ibn Afra and Muadh ibn Amr but was finally killed by Abdullah ibn Masud.

            Abu Jahal was known as Abu Hakam (“Father of Wisdom”)


            Additional Information:

            battle Of Badar was fought 2nd Hijri.

            Muslim Participated in 313 and Kufars 1000.

            Muslim Martyred: 14

            376

            Who was made governor of North Yemen by Holy Prophet?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Musa Ashri
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Hazrat Abu Musa al-Ash'ari was at various times governor of Basra and Kufa and was involved in the early Muslim conquest of Persia.
            377

            Who killed Musailamah al Kazzab?

            Correct Answer: Wahshi ibn Harb
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Musaylimah was killed in the Battle of Yamama by Wahshi ibn Harb,

            the same man who had killed Muhammad's uncle, Hazrat Hamza, in the battle of Uhud before his conversion to Islam.
            378

            What was the title of Hazrat Talha (R.A)?

            Correct Answer: Naqeeb-e-Ansar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Abu Talha Ansari (RA) was badly injured in the battle of Uhad.

             

            379

            How many Muslims were martyred in the battle of Trench____?

            Correct Answer: 6
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Battle of Trench is also known as battle of Confederates.

            Battle of Trench is also known as battle of Ditch.


            380

            Before Islam most of the Arab's worshipped:

            Correct Answer: Idols
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hubal was the largest idol in Kaaba before Islam.

            Religion in pre-Islamic Arabia was a mix of polytheism, Christianity, Judaism, and Iranian religions.

            Gods and goddesses were worshipped at local shrines, such as the Kaaba in Mecca.

            381

            “Dua e Qunoot” is recited in____prayer?

            Correct Answer: Witr
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Witr is an Islamic prayer that is performed at night after Isha or before fajr. Witr has an odd number of raka'at prayed in pairs, with the final raka'ah prayed separately. Therefore, as little as one raka'ah can be prayed, and eleven at most.
            382

            How Much Takbeers were recieted by Moazin In Azan ?

            Correct Answer: 6
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Bilal ibn Rabah is considered to have been the first mu'azzin, chosen by Hazrat Muhammad himself.

            Born: 580 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia

            Died: March 2, 640 AD, Damascus, Syria

            383

            What was the miracle given to Hazrat Isa (AS) by Allah?

            Correct Answer: Reviving the Dead
            Show explanation

            Hazrat Isa (As) was Born Bethlehem

            Hazrat Isa (As) is known Rohullah

            Injeel book was revealed on Hazrat Isa (As)

            384

            Mirror was invented by Prophet?

            Correct Answer: Sulaiman (A.S)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Boat was invented by NUH as.

            Bridge was invented by YUSUF as.

            Comb was invented by IBRAHIM as.

            Mirror was invented by SULAIMAN as.

            وحی کی لغوی معنی کیا ہے ؟

            387

            Which of the following Gazwa is known as the last Gazwa of Holy Prophet?

            Correct Answer: Tabook
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Battle of Tabuk is known as the last Gazwa of Holy Prophet

            Ghazwa Tabook is fought against: Romans

            Ghazwa e Tabook was fought in the year: 9 Hijri

            initiated by Muhammad in October 630 AD.

            It took place During Rajab and Sha'ban in the region of Tabuk

            20 days Hazrat Muhammad PBUH stayed during Tabook Battle

            ***

            ND29-1-2023


            388

            A book on collection of Ahadis of Sahih Bukhari is

            Correct Answer: Imam bukhari
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sahih al-Bukhari is a hadith collection and a book of sunnah compiled by the Uzbek scholar Muḥammad ibn Ismā'īl al-Bukhārī 

            Sahih Al Bukhari contains around 2,600 ahadith without repetitions

            and 7,563 ahadith with repetitions.

            It is an important book in Islamic literature

            part of the Kutub al-Sittah,

            97 chapters.


            389

            Holy Quran revealed on which Prophet

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Muhammad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Holy Quran revealed on Hazrat Muhammad

            Iqra was the first revealed word in the Holy Quran

            5 verses of Surah Al-Alaq were first revealed by the Holy Prophet (PBUH)

            Surah Alaq was the first verse in the Holy Quran revealed

            Surah Al Nasr was last revealed

            Quran was orally revealed by God,

            Quran was revealed through the archangel Gabriel (Jibril),

            23 قرآن کا نزول کتنے سالوں میں مکمل ہوا: سال

            Quran revelation was beginning on 22 December 609 CE,

            Quran was orally revealed when Muhammad was 40 years old

            Quran was concluded in 632, the year of Muhammad's death

            393

            The age of hazrat Ali was:

            Correct Answer: 61
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The age of hazrat Ali  was 61
            • **
            • At age 10, ʿAlī accepted Islam,
            • Hazrat Ali die: at 61 years (599 AD–661 AD)
            • Hazrat Ali, the cousin and son-in-law of Prophet Muhammad is believed to have been the first male to adopt Islam
            • **
            • Ali was killed in the mosque of Kufa by the sword of one of the Kharijites, Ibn Muljam Moradi.
            • Buried outside the city of Kufa.
            • His shrine and the city of Najaf were built around his tomb

            394

            Ayat al kursi is part of which Surah in the Holy Quran?

            Correct Answer: AL Baqarah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            آيَةُ ٱلْكُرْسِيِّ, Ayat Al-Kursi is the 255th verse of the 2nd chapter of the Quran, Al-Baqara (Q2:255).

            ٱللَّهُ لَاۤ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلۡحَیُّ ٱلۡقَیُّومُۚ لَا تَأۡخُذُهُۥ سِنَةࣱ وَلَا نَوۡمࣱۚ لَّهُۥ مَا فِی ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰ⁠تِ وَمَا فِی ٱلۡأَرۡضِۗ مَن ذَا ٱلَّذِی یَشۡفَعُ عِندَهُۥۤ إِلَّا بِإِذۡنِهِۦۚ یَعۡلَمُ مَا بَیۡنَ أَیۡدِیهِمۡ وَمَا خَلۡفَهُمۡۖ وَلَا یُحِیطُونَ بِشَیۡءࣲ مِّنۡ عِلۡمِهِۦۤ إِلَّا بِمَا شَاۤءَۚ وَسِعَ كُرۡسِیُّهُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَ ٰ⁠تِ وَٱلۡأَرۡضَۖ وَلَا یَـُٔودُهُۥ حِفۡظُهُمَاۚ وَهُوَ ٱلۡعَلِیُّ ٱلۡعَظِیمُ 

            ND5-5-2023



            395

            مسجد نبوی کہاں واقع ہے؟

            Correct Answer: مدینہ
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Al-Masjid an-Nabawi is the second mosque built by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in Medina

            مسجد نبوی is a mosque in the city of Madina in the Saudi region of the Hejaz

            مسجد نبوی the second largest mosque and the holiest site in Islam

            The construction of مسجد نبوی started in: 1 Hijir

            مسجد نبوی was built by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) himself, next to the house in 622 AD (first Hijri).

            On 29th Hijrah was مسجد نبوی expanded

            Height: 104 m

            Burials: Abu Bakr, Umar

            Minaret(s): 10

            399

            Qamoos temple was conquered by Hazrat Ali (R.A) during which war?

            Correct Answer: Battle of Khaybar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Battle of Khaybar was fought in the year 628 between Muslims and the Jews living in the oasis of Khaybar, located 150 kilometers (93 mi) from Medina, Saudi Arabia. 

            This battle was fought in Sevent Hijri.

            Muslims won the battle.




            400

            Name the last Sariya__?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Saad bin Abi Waqas (رضہ)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hazrat Saʿd was the seventh person to embrace Islam, which he did at the age of seventeen.
            Hazrat Saʿd Born:             595 AD, Mecca, Saudi Arabia
            Hazrat Saʿd Died:             674 AD, Al-Baqi', Medina, Saudi Arabia
            Hazrat Saʿd Full name:             Saʿd ibn Abī Waqqās
            401

            Name the prophet who was married to Rachel__________?

            Correct Answer: Hazrat Yaqoob (A.S)
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Soon after his arrival in Harran, Jacob fell in love with the “beautiful and lovely” Rachel, daughter of his cousin Laban.
            Laban warmly welcomed him to his family, but asked a steep price for Rachel's hand in marriage: Jacob would first have to work as             a shepherd for seven years, tending Laban's flocks.

            uhad is in which direction from Madina?

            Correct Answer: North
            Show explanation

            Correct Answer: 25
